Pak Study MCQs · pages 401-450

Sharpen your Pak Study recall

Tap a card to reveal the explanation. Take it one seal at a time.

500 questions in this set
1

Pakistan launched which program to boost economic sector for the period from 2024 to 2029?

Correct Answer: Uraan Pakistan
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Uraan Pakistan, officially titled National Economic Transformation Plan 2024–29
  • It aims to drive sustainable growth and modernize Pakistan's economy 
    • Launched by Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif 
  • It is a five-year economic strategy
  • Launched on 31 December 2024.


***

Last updated by NS on 28April 2026

2

In Dec 2024, PM Shehbaz Sharif launched ‘Uraan Pakistan’ transformation plan to boost which sector?

Correct Answer: Economic Sector
Show explanation
Explanation

Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif launched the ‘Uraan Pakistan, Homegrown National Economic Plan’ outlining the National Economic Transformation Plan for 2024-29.

Launched on 31 December 2024.

***

3

What is the motto of Supreme Court of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: So judge between the people in truth
Show explanation
Explanation

The motto of the Supreme Court of Pakistan is "So judge between the people in truth" (Quran 38:26).

The Supreme Court of Pakistan is the apex court in the judicial hierarchy of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan..

***

4

Which province in Pakistan implemented Article 25-A first?

Correct Answer: Sindh
Show explanation
Explanation

Sindh was the first province in Pakistan to implement Article 25-A.

  • Article 25-A ensures free and compulsory education for children aged 5 to 16 years.
  • Legislation Timeline:
    • ICT: 2012
    • Sindh: 2013
    • Punjab: 2014
    • Balochistan: 2014
    • KP: 2017

    Last updated questionon by NA 06 May 2026

    5

    War of Independence 1857 was officially declared ended in _____?

    Correct Answer: November 1858
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The War of Independence of 1857 was officially declared ended by the British government on November 1, 1858, with the proclamation of Queen Victoria's rule over India.
    • The Indian Rebellion of 1857, also known as the First War of Independence.
    • It was began on May 10, 1857, at Meerut as a sepoy mutiny.
    6

    How many got Nishan-e-Haider in 1971 war?

    Correct Answer: 5
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Nishan-e-Haider literally means Emblem of Lion.

    1. Rashid Minhas No. 2 Fighter Conversion Unit, Pakistan Air Force Pilot Officer Indo-Pakistani War of 1971 20 August 1971
    2. Muhammad Akram 4 Frontier Force Regiment, Pakistan Army Major Indo-Pakistani War of 1971 5 December 1971
    3. Shabbir Sharif 6 Frontier Force Regiment, Pakistan Army Major Indo-Pakistani War of 1971 6 December 1971
    4. Muhammad Hussain 20 Lancers (Armoured Corps), Pakistan Army Sowar Indo-Pakistani War of 1971 10 December 1971
    5. Muhammad Mahfuz Janjua 15 Punjab Regiment, Pakistan Army Lance naik Indo-Pakistani War of 1971 17 December 1971
    7

    Under Nationalization Policy of Zulifqar Ali Bhutto ______?

    Correct Answer: All these were controlled
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Zulfikar Ali Bhutto (PPP) announced the nationalization of major industries on January 2, 1972.

    These industries included iron and steel, heavy engineering, heavy electricals, petrochemicals, cement, and public utilities.

    8

    Which of the following rulers banned music in his region?

    Correct Answer: Aurangzeb
    Show explanation
    Explanation
  • Aurangzeb's full name was Abul Muzaffar Muhi-ud-Din Muhammad Aurangzeb.
  • He is also known as Aurangzeb Alamgir.
  • He banned music and dance in his kingdom in 1668 due to his dislike for them.
    • 9

    Atta Shad and Isha Shamin are notable poets of ______?

    Correct Answer: Balochi
    Show explanation
    Explanation
  • Atta Shad and Isha Shamin are renowned poets known for their significant contributions to Balochi literature and poetry.
  • They played a pivotal role in enriching Balochi language, culture, and literary heritage.
    • 10

    In which of the following cities Pakistan Command and Staff College is located?

    Correct Answer: Quetta
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pakistan Command and Staff College is located in Quetta. 

    The Command and Staff College is the Pakistan Army's staff college for mid-career army officers.

    As the Army Staff College it was initially established in 1905 at Deolali and later shifted to Quetta in 1907, Baluchistan.
    11

    When the Stock market is rising it is called?

    Correct Answer: Bullish
    Show explanation
    Explanation
  • A rising stock market is referred to as "bullish".
  • It indicating investor confidence and optimism about future gains.
  • The term originates from the upward thrust of a bull's horns, symbolizing an upward trend.
    • 12

    A Sindhiology department has been established in the University of _____?

    Correct Answer: Sindh University Jamshoro
    Show explanation
    Explanation
  • Institute of Sindhology: Established in 1962 by the University of Sindh as the "Sindhi Academy."
  • Headquarters in Jamshoro, Pakistan.
  • Research on Sindh's history, society, culture, and literature.
  • Key People: Nabi Bux Khan Baloch, Muhammad Hanif Siddiqui, Pir Hassam-ud-Din Rashidi, Ghulam Ali Allana, Muhammad Qasim Maka, Saeed Ahmed Mangi.
    • 13

    Who gave a proposal to form a new Political party for the Muslims of India?

    Correct Answer: Nawab Saleem Ullah Khan
    Show explanation
    Explanation
  • During the 1906 All India Muslim Education Conference in Dhaka, Khwaja Salimullah proposed forming a political party for Muslims.
  • This led to the creation of the All-India Muslim League to safeguard Muslim interests in British India.
    • 14

    Who established Federal Security Force?

    Correct Answer: Zulifqar Ali bhutto
    Show explanation
    Explanation
  • The Federal Security Force (FSF) was established in 1972 by President Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto.
  • It functioned as a paramilitary force under the direct control of the Government of Pakistan.
    • 15

    JAMSHED MARKER distinguished himself in the field of ______?

    Correct Answer: Diplomacy
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Jamsheed Marker, was a Pakistani diplomat and a former cricket commentator.

    The recipient of Hilal-e-Imtiaz,

    he was associated with diplomatic career for over 42 years.

    16

    When was National Anthem of Pakistan played on the radio for the first time?

    Correct Answer: 1954
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    About National Anthem

    National Anthem was written by Hafeez Jalandhari in 1952 

    the music was produced by Ahmad G. Chagla in 1949.

    It was officially adopted as Pakistan's national anthem on 13 August 1954.

    Ahmed Rushdi recorded the National Anthem of Pakistan in 1954.

    After 68 years Pakistan's National Anthem recorded

    15 lines in the National Anthem of Pakistan

    48 Words in the National Anthem of Pakistan

    The National Anthem of Pakistan was played for the first time in front of Raza Shah Rahalvi of Iran

    In the National Anthem of Pakistan, " Jan-i-Istaqbal" stands for the Symbol of ambitions.

    the first national anthem of Pakistan was played on Pakistan's national radio on 14 August 1947

    80 sec is the duration of the National Anthem of Pakistan

    which language is Pakistan's national Anthem written in? Persian

    ND7-5-2023

    ***

    ND7-5-2023


    17

    The British Government announced annulment of partition of Bengal after ______ years?

    Correct Answer: 6
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Ø  On the visiting His Majesty King George V to Indo-Pakistan and holding of Darbar at Delhi on 12th December 1911 the partition of Bengal was cancelled.

    Ø  ****

    Ø  Partition of Bangal Announced on 19 July 1905 by Lord Curzon for Administrative requirements

    Ø  Congress protest against Partition of Bangal

    18

    In March 1920, Gandhi started ______?

    Correct Answer: Non-Cooperation Movement
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In March 1920, Mahatma Gandhi issued a manifesto declaring a doctrine of the non-violent non-cooperation movement.

    Gandhi, through this manifesto, wanted people to: Adopt swadeshi principles.

    The movement focused on three key issues:

    1. The Jallianwala Bagh massacre
    2. The Khilafat wrongs
    3. The demand for Swaraj (self-rule)
    19

    OGRA is the regulatory body for _____?

    Correct Answer: Oil and Gas
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    OGRA stands for Oil and Gas Regulatory Authority.

    It is the regulatory body responsible for overseeing the oil and gas sector in Pakistan.

    It was established in 2002.

    It to ensure a fair and transparent regulatory framework for the exploration, production, transportation, and distribution of oil and gas resources.

    20

    The book "Zulfi Bhutto of Pakistan" was written by ______?

    Correct Answer: Stanley Wolpert
    Show explanation
    Explanation
  • Title: Zulfi Bhutto of Pakistan.
  • Author: Stanley Wolpert.
  • Language: English.
  • Genre: Biography.
  • Publisher: Oxford University Press.
  • Publication Date: July 1, 1993.
    • 21

    Agartala Conspiracy Case opened in which year?

    Correct Answer: 1968-69
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The case is officially called State vs. Sheikh Mujibur Rahman

    The Agartala Conspiracy Case was filed in January 1969 – March 1969 

    As a sedition case by the government of Pakistan against Sheikh Mujibur Rahman

    GoalsResignation of President Ayub Khan, Withdrawal of Agartala Conspiracy Case and Autonomy for East Pakistan Six Points
    MethodsProtest march
    Resulted inAyub Khan's resignation
    Yahya Khan becomes president
    Parties
    Government of Pakistan
    Pakistan Army    		Awami League
    Lead figures

    Muhammad Ayub Khan

    Sheikh Mujibur Rahman

    22

    To whom Lord Mountbatten succeeded as Governor General of India?

    Correct Answer: Lord Wavell
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Lord Mountbatten replaced Lord Wavell as the viceroy of India in 1947.

    Lord Mountbatten: The last Viceroy of India

    Lord Mountbatten passed away on 27 August 1979

    In March 1947, Mountbatten was appointed Viceroy of India and oversaw the Partition of British India into India and Pakistan. 

    The legislature representatives of the Indian National Congress, the Muslim League, and the Sikh community came to an agreement with Lord Mountbatten through "The Indian Independence Act of 1947"

    This plan is known as the 3 June Plan or Mountbatten Plan.

    ND28-4-2023


    23

    Muhammadan Educational Conference was established by Sir Syed Ahmed Khan in which year?

    Correct Answer: 1886
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Muhammadan Educational Conference, was established  in Aligarh in 1886

    History:

    The Al India Muhammadan Educational Conference promotes modern education for the Muslim community in India.

    It was founded by Sir Syed Ahmed Khan 

    It was the origin of the All-Indian Muslim League.

    Muslim League was born in the 20th session of the All India Muhammad Educational Conference, Established by Syed Ahmed Khan in Aligarh in 1886.

    24

    Mughal Empire Aurangzeb Alamgir ruled the subcontinent from _____?

    Correct Answer: 1658-1707
    Show explanation
    Explanation
  • Alamgir, sixth emperor of the Mughal Empire.
  • Reign: 1658–1707 (49 years).
  • Birth: November 3, 1618, in Dhod, Malwa.
  • Death: March 3, 1707, in Bhingar, Aurangabad Subah.
  • Third son of Shah Jahan and Mumtaz Mahal.
    • 25

    Who was Al-Khwarizmi?

    Correct Answer: Mathematician
    Show explanation
    Explanation
  • Full Name: Muḥammad ibn Mūsā Al-Khwārizmī.
  • Field: Muslim mathematician and astronomer.
  • Contributions: Introduced Hindu-Arabic numerals and algebra into European mathematics.
  • Born: Khwarazm.
  • Died: Baghdad, Iraq.
  • Era: Islamic Golden Age (Abbasid era).
    • 26

    Board of Revenue of Umayyad reign was called _____?

    Correct Answer: Diwan al-Kharaj
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Diwan al-Kharaj was the board of revenue during the Umayyad reign.

    It responsible for collecting taxes such as land revenue and overseeing financial matters of the state.

    27

    The length of Khojak Tunnel is _____?

    Correct Answer: 3.9 KM
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Khojak Tunnel

    Location

    Balochistan

    Length

    3.9 KM

    Connects

    Qila Abdullah with Chaman

    Longest Railway Tunnel of

    Pakistan

    Also known as

    Shela Bagh Tunnel

    Located

    West of Quetta

    Construction started

    1888

    Completed

    1891


  • Recognition: The State Bank of Pakistan acknowledged the Khojak Tunnel's significance.
  • Five-Rupee Note: A picture of the tunnel was featured on the five-rupee note.
  • Circulation Period: The note was in circulation from 1976 to 2005.
    • 28

    Who was appointed as Pakistan's first woman ambassador?

    Correct Answer: Ra'ana Liaquat Ali Khan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Ra'ana Liaquat Ali Khan was the first woman to be appointed as Pakistan's ambassador, in 1954.

    She represented Pakistan in the Netherlands until 1961.

    29

    Which day Pakistani celebrate on 5th February?

    Correct Answer: Kashmir Solidarity Day
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Kashmir Solidarity Day or Kashmir Day is a national holiday observed in Pakistan 

    Date: February 5

    The Event Length: 1 Day

    It is Related to the Kashmir Conflict

    It was Started by: Jamaat-e-Islami Pakistan in 1990

    It was first proposed by Qazi Hussain Ahmad 

    In 1991, the then-Prime Minister of Pakistan Nawaz Sharif called for a "Kashmir Solidarity Day Strike"


    Addtional information:


    یوم یکجہتی کشمیر 5 فروری کو ہندوستان کے زیر انتظام جموں و کشمیر اور کشمیریوں کے ساتھ پاکستان کی حمایت اور اتحاد کو ظاہر کرنے کے لیے منایا جاتا ہے۔

    30

    Which was the favourite game of Quaid-I-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah?

    Correct Answer: Billiards
    Show explanation
    Explanation
  • Favourite Sports: Quaid-i-Azam enjoyed billiards and cricket.
  • He was a member of the Orient Club in India, where he also played chess.
  • Famous Image: An iconic picture shows him playing billiards.
  • Favourite Food: His favorite dish was curry and rice.
    • 31

    Pakistan won a gold medal for the first time in Olympics in _____ ?

    Correct Answer: 1960
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pakistan won its first Olympic Games gold medal in 1960


    By defeating India in the final of the men's hockey competition.


    The Summer Olympics were held in Rome, Italy.


    Competitors: 44 (all men)


    Took part in 35 events in 7 sports


    Medals
    Ranked 20th

    Gold
    1
    Silver
    0
    Bronze
    1
    Total
    2


    Pakistan has won a total of 04 gold medals in the Olympic Games so far

    •  Rome in 1960.

     Mexico, held in 1968.

     Angeles, USA, held in 1984.

    Javelin Throw in Paris held in 2024.

     *****

    32

    Who presented the Nehru Report?

    Correct Answer: Motilal Nehru
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Nehru Report was presented in 1928.

    The Nehru Report presented by Motilal Nehru, with his son Jawaharlal Nehru.

    33

    Presidential form of government in Pakistan was introduced under the Constitution of ____?

    Correct Answer: 1962
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    The Constitution of 1962 envisaged a Federal State with a Presidential form of government,

    with the National Assembly at the center and the Provincial Assemblies in the Provinces
    ***
    The Constitution of 1962 was promulgated by President Ayub Khan
    on 1 March 1962 and finally came into effect on 8 June 1962.

    Urdu and Bengali Was The Official Language 

    Ayoub Khan was made chief of martial law after this abrogation

    Ayoub Khan enforced the new constitution on 8th June 1962

    Yahiya Khan abrogated the 1962 constitution and became CMLA
    34

    The first-ever women police station was established in ______?

    Correct Answer: Islamabad
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pakistan’s first women police station inaugurated by late Prime Minister Benazir Bhutto in Islamabad on January 25, 1994

    پاکستان کے پہلے خواتین پولیس اسٹیشن کا افتتاح وزیر اعظم بے نظیر بھٹو نے 25 جنوری 1994 کو اسلام آباد میں کیا

    35

    Which one is the largest medal of bravery of Pakistan Police?

    Correct Answer: Quaid-e-Azam Medal
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Quaid-e-Azam Police Medal is the highest bravery award in Pakistan Police, established in 1989.

    It is awarded for extraordinary acts of courage and bravery in the line of duty.


    Additional Information:

    Nishan-e-Haider (highest military honor)

    Hilal-e-Jurat (military award for gallantry)

    Nishan-e-Pakistan (civilian award for outstanding service)

    Sitara-e-Pakistan (civilian award for outstanding service)

    Tamgha-e-Pakistan (civilian award for dedicated service)

    36

    Dr. Fahmida Mirza, the Speaker of the National Assembly, was elected as MNA from ______?

    Correct Answer: Sindh
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Dr. Fahmida Mirza was born in December 20, 1956.

    She is a medical doctor, agriculturist and businesswoman from Hyderabad, Sindh, Pakistan.

    She was elected as the first female Speaker of the National Assembly of Pakistan on March 19, 2008.

    37

    NUML (National University of Modern Languages) is in _____?

    Correct Answer: Islamabad
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    NUML (National University of Modern Languages) is located in Islamabad, Pakistan, and serves as its main campus.

    NUML has regional campuses in cities like Lahore, Karachi, Peshawar, and Quetta.

    It was established in 1969 and gained university status in 2000.

    38

    Baglihar Dam, a disputed dam between India and Pakistan, has been built in _____?

    Correct Answer: Held Kashmir
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Baglihar Dam, built on the Chenab River in the Doda district of Jammu & Kashmir.
      • It is the first power project executed by the Jammu and Kashmir Power Development Corporation.
      • It is conceived in 1992 and approved in 1996.
      • It is construction began in 1999.
      • The dam opened in 2008 with an estimated cost of US$1 billion.
      • It was commissioned in two stages: Stage I in 2008-09 and Stage II in 2015-16.
      39

      Gwadar is located in the _____?

      Correct Answer: Balochistan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Gwadar Port is located on the Arabian Sea in Balochistan, Pakistan.

      It was opened in 2002.

      Pakistan purchased Gwadar from Oman on September 8, 1958.

      It was officially became part of Pakistan on December 8, 1958.

      Gwadar was under Omani rule for 174 years, from 1783 to 1958.

      40

      Ahmed Shaheed Brelvi is buried in which city|town?

      Correct Answer: Balakot
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • Full Name: Syed Ahmad Barelvi (Sayyid Ahmad Shaheed).
    • Birth: 29 November 1786, Rae Bareli, Kingdom of Oudh.
    • Death: 6 May 1831, during the Battle of Balakot (aged 44).
    • Role: Indian Muslim revivalist from Rae Bareli, Uttar Pradesh.
    • Legacy: Known for his efforts in Islamic revival and martyrdom at Balakot.
      • 41

      Which of the following leaders took part in Presidential Election?

      Correct Answer: Mohtarma Fatima Jinnah
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • Mohtarma Fatima Jinnah contested the 1965 presidential election as the leader of the Combined Opposition Parties (COP).
    • She ran against Ayub Khan, who represented the Pakistan Muslim League.
      • 42

      After The assassination of Pakistan's first Prime Minister Liaquat Ali Khan, the post of Prime Minister was filled by?

      Correct Answer: Khawaja Nazimuddin
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Liaquat Ali Khan was assassinated on October 16, 1951, in Rawalpindi.

      Khawaja Nazimuddin took office in 1951 as PM of Pakistan.

      Khawaja Nazimuddin left the office when Governor-General Malik Ghulam Muhammad dissolved his government in 1953.

      43

      پاکستان میں رائج الوقت قوانین کو اسلامی سانچے میں ڈھالنے کے لیے کون سا ادارہ قائم کیا گیا ہے؟

      Correct Answer: اسلامی نظریاتی کونسل
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      پاکستان میں رائج الوقت قوانین کو اسلامی سانچے میں ڈھالنے کے لیے اسلامی نظریاتی کونسل قائم کی گئی تھی۔

      اس کونسل کا مقصد مختلف قوانین کو اسلام کی تعلیمات کے مطابق جائزہ لینا اور ان میں اصلاحات کی تجویز دینا ہے۔







      44

      Allama Dr. Sarfraz Hussain Naeemi Shaheed was awarded _______?

      Correct Answer: Hilal-i-Shujaat
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • Allama Dr. Sarfraz Naeemi Shaheed was posthumously awarded Hilal-i-Shujaat for bravery.
    • On 15 June 2009, PM Yousaf Raza Gilani announced the award, with recognition for promoting peace and combating extremism.
    • Date of Birth: 16 February 1948.
    • Date of Death: 12 June 2009.
      • 45

      Bahadur Shah Zafar was exiled to which city?

      Correct Answer: Rangoon
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Mughal King Bahadur Shah Zafar was exiled to Rangoon, Burma after his defeat.
      • The British exiled him to Rangoon in 1858 after the Indian Rebellion of 1857.
      • He was the last Mughal emperor of India.
      • He was reigning from 1837 to 1857.
      • He was also a poet, musician and calligrapher.

      47

      Which among the following is not a Kharif Crop?

      Correct Answer: Wheat
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Major Kharif crops are:

      1.       Rice 

      2.       Sugarcane

      3.       Cotton

      4.       Maze

      5.       Bajra

      6.       Jawar

      When the period of the Kharif crop starts and when end?

      Strat: April-June,      

      End:  Sept-Oct

      while

      When does the period of the Rabi crop start and when end?

      These crops are planted in winter from October to December

      and harvested in summer from March to May

      Which are Pakistan's major seasons for the agricultural crop?

      Kharif & Rabi

      ND23-2-2023

      48

      Which is the largest river in Balochistan?

      Correct Answer: Hungol river
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hingol River  is located in the Makran region, in the Gwadar District of southeastern Balochistan Province

      The Hingol River is 350 miles (560 km) long, the longest in Balochistan.
      *****

      How many rivers flow in Punjab Province?

      5

      How many rivers flow in Sindh Province?

      4

      How many rivers flow in Balochistan Province?

      7

      How many rivers flow in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Province?

      8

      How many rivers are present in Pakistan?

      24

      ND11-2-2023




      49

      Who was the first Punjabi language poet?

      Correct Answer: Baba Farid
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Baba Fareed or  was born in 1188 (573 AH) in Kothewal
      •  Baba Fareed was a Sunni Muslim and was one of the founding fathers of the Chishti Sufi order.
      • The Shrine of Baba Farid is a 13th-century Sufi shrine located in Pakpattan, Pakistan
      • Baba Fareed Shaker Ganj' was the first poet of: Punjabi
      • ******
      50

      KANYUP (The Karachi Nuclear Power Plant) was provided to Pakistan by ____?

      Correct Answer: Canada
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      KANUPP stands for Karachi Nuclear Power Plant

      it is a large commercial nuclear power plant located at Paradise Point in Karachi Sindh 

      it is Pakistan's first nuclear energy plant

      it is composed of three commercial nuclear power plants

      K1: 1 August 1966 (support from Canada) 

      K2: 20 August 2015 (investment provided by China and IAEA)

      K3: 31 May 2016 (investment provided by China and IAEA)

      51

      Pakistan became member of ILO _______?

      Correct Answer: 31 October 1947
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The ILO was created in 1919, as part of the Treaty of Versailles which ended World War 

      International Labour Organization (ILO) declared World Day Against Child Labour on 2002

      Pakistan has been an important and active member of the ILO since 31 October 1947.

      Ratified 36 ILO Conventions.

      The ILO Office was established in Pakistan in 1970.

      ****

      Headquarters: Geneva, Switzerland

      Parent organization: United Nations

      Founder: Paris Peace Conference

      ND23-4-2023


      RY29012023

      rk4323


      52

      Liaquat - Nehru pact was announced at Delhi in ______?

      Correct Answer: April 8, 1950
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The Liaquat-Nehru Pact, also called the Delhi Pact.
      • It was signed on April 8, 1950.
      • It was an agreement between:
        • India and Pakistan to address minority rights.
      • The pact was signed by :
        • PM Jawaharlal Nehru of India 
        • PM Liaquat Ali Khan of Pakistan.


      Last updated by NA on 27 April 2026

      53

      Who presided over the Wavell Plan?

      Correct Answer: Lord Wavell
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Wavell Plan was first presented at the Shimla Conference in 1945.

      It was named after Viceroy of India, Lord Wavell.

      54

      Ibn Battuta, the famous Muslim explorer who came to India during regime of Mohammad Bin Tughlaq, belonged to which country?

      Correct Answer: Morocco
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Battutah, commonly known as Ibn Battuta

      Born: February 24, 1304, Tangier, Morocco

      Died: 1369, Marrakesh, Morocco

      Place of burial: Tomb of Ibn Battuta, Tangier, Morocco

      Ibn Battuta visited India during the reign of Muhammad bin Tughlaq


      Ibn Battuta was: a traveler

      Ibn Battuta belonged: Morocco

      Over a period of thirty years, Ibn Battuta visited most of the:

      North Africa,

      the Middle East,

      Central Asia,

      South Asia,

      Southeast Asia,

      China,

      and the Iberian Peninsula

      55

      Muhammad Khan Junejo, PM of Pakistan, left his office on which date?

      Correct Answer: 29 May 1988
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Muhammad Khan Junejo, the tenth Prime Minister of Pakistan, left his office on May 29, 1988.

      He was dismissed by President Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq due to their conflicting views on governance and military control.

      He was succeeded by general elections held in 1988.

      Why Muslim observed "Day of Deliverance"?

      Correct Answer: End of Congress Ministries
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • The "Day of Deliverance" was declared by Muslim League President Muhammad Ali Jinnah.
    • On December 2, 1939, Jinnah appealed to Indian Muslims to celebrate December 22, 1939.
    • It marked freedom from Congress during the Indian independence movement.

      Dars-e-Nizamia was formalized during the reign of Emperor Humayun in the Mughal Empire.

      Dars-i Nizami is a study curriculum or system used in many Islamic institutions (madrassas) and Darul Ulooms,


      They can now also be found in parts of "

      • South Africa
      • Canadagr
      • the United States
      • the Caribbean 
      • the UK.


      Dars-i Nizami system was developed by Nizamuddin Sihalivi

      Which are the Boundary Lines between India and Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: All of these
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The boundaries between India and Pakistan include the 24th Parallel, Line of Control (LoC), and the Working Boundary.

      24th parallel line is also known as Sir Creek.

      The Radcliffe Line between India and Pakistan that came into effect on August 17, 1947.

      The line was named after Sir Cyril Radcliffe.

      The Elections for Assemblies were held in Oct, 2002 when the Elections for Senate were held?

      Correct Answer: February 2003
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • The elections for the Senate of Pakistan were held on February 24, 2003.
    • The newly elected Senators took office on March 12, 2003.
    • General elections were held in Pakistan on 10 October 2002.
    • These elections elected the 12th National Assembly and four Provincial Assemblies.
    • They were conducted under the military government of Pervez Musharraf.

      According to the Government of India Act of 1935, elections in the subcontinent were conducted in which year?

      Correct Answer: 1937
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Government of India Act of 1935 was passed by the British Parliament in August 1935.

      But the elections were not conducted immediately.

      The act came into force in April 1937.

      The first elections under the act were held the same year.

      Correct Answer: Montgomery

    • It was later renamed Montgomery.

      Ajodhanpur is the old name of ______?

      Correct Answer: Pakpattan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Its original name was "Ajodhan," later renamed Pakpattan by Hazrat Baba Fariduddin Ganj Shakar.

      Pakpattan is the capital of Pakpattan District in Punjab, Pakistan.

      It is the 48th largest city of Pakistan by population as per the 2017 census.

      Correct Answer: Changaiz Khan

    • This invasion was a result of trade disputes and the execution of Mongol merchants by Khwarzim's ruler.

      Dyarchy was introduced in the subcontinent in the year _____?

      Correct Answer: 1919
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Dyarchy particularly referred to the system of shared rule in British India established by the Government of India Acts 1919 

      Dyarchy means the double system of government in which two heads are responsible for administration.

      Dyarchy was introduced as a constitutional reform by Edwin Samuel Montagu and Lord Chelmsford 

      The Government of India Act of 1935 ended the system of Diarchy and established 'provincial autonomy'.

    • The track gauge is 1,676 millimeters (5 ft 6 in).
    • Current operating speed is 30–115 kilometers per hour (19–71 miles per hour).
    • A proposed speed of 140 kilometers per hour (87 miles per hour).

      The oldest Hydro-electric Project in Pakistan is _____?

      Correct Answer: Malakand Dargai
      Show explanation

      It is located on Sawat Canal, Malakand Division.

      Opening date: July 1938

      The 70-year-old powerhouse (Malakand-I) was badly damaged due to a fire incident on 11 December 2006.

      The Quaid-e-Azam stayed during his last illness in 1948 in ________ ?

      Show explanation

      It is located in Ziarat, Balochistan, Pakistan.

      He spent the last two months and ten days of his life.

      He died on September 11, 1948, in Karachi.

      He was the founder and first governor-general (1947–48) of Pakistan.

      Which of the following is not the "cash crop"?

      Correct Answer: Wheat
      Show explanation

      1. Cotton
      2. Wheat
      3. Rice
      4. Sugarcane
      5. Maize

      It followed by rice, which is the second main staple food crop and a major export commodity.

      94

      C.R Formula was given by _______?

      Correct Answer: Raj Gopal Acharia
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      It was a proposal formulated by Chakravarti Rajagopalachari

      C. R. formula is also known as the Rajaji formula 

      In 1942, Chakravarti Rajagopalachar opposed the Congress party by differing against the Quit India Movement and left the party 

      C. R.'s formula solves the political deadlock between the All India Muslim League and the Indian National Congress on the independence of British India.

      The CR formula acted as the basis for the negotiations. Gandhi and Jinnah met in September 1944 to ease the deadlock.


      ND14-10-2023

      Located in Thatta.

      The city ruins lie on the N-5 National Highway, east of Karachi.

      Pakistan Resolution 1940, was seconded from Sindh by?

      Correct Answer: Sir Abdullah Haroon
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      It was moved in the general session by A. K. Fazlul Huq, the Chief Minister of undivided Bengal.

      It was seconded by:

      • Chaudhry Khaliquzzaman from the United Provinces
      • Zafar Ali Khan from Punjab
      • Sardar Aurangzeb Khan from the North-West Frontier Province
      • Sir Abdullah Haroon from Sindh

      When Water Apportionment Accord was signed Between provinces of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: 1991
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • The Water Apportionment Accord (WAA) was signed by provinces on 16 March 1991.
    • It was approved by the Council of Common Interests (CCI) on 21 March 1991.
    • Clause 13 of WAA recognized the need to establish the Indus River System Authority (IRSA).
    • IRSA was created to implement the Accord.

      Kargil is in ______?

      Correct Answer: Held Kashmir
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Kargil is located in the Ladakh region, which is part of Indian-administered (Held) Kashmir.

      The Kargil War was fought between India and Pakistan on1999

      Kargil War fought from the month of May to July 1999

      The Kargil War is also known as the Kargil Conflict but in India, this conflict is known as Operation Vijay

      The Kargil War was fought in the Kargil district of Kashmir and elsewhere along the Line of Control (LoC).

      Kargil's Issue was stopped by the intervention of the United States of America (USA).

      Strength: India: 30,000 = Pakistan: 5,000

      Captain Karnal Sher Khan Shaheed Nishan-e-Haider was known as the hero of the Kargil war

      General Pervez Musharraf was the Chief of the Army Staff of the Pakistani army during the Kargil War in 1999.

      Gen VP Malik was the Chief of the Army Staff of the Indian army during the Kargil war in 1999.

      How many gates did the Old Walled City of Lahore have?

      Correct Answer: 13
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Old Walled City of Lahore had 13 gates built by Mughal emperor Akbar:

      1. Akbari Gate
      2. Bhati Gate
      3. Delhi Gate
      4. Kashmiri Gate
      5. Lohari Gate
      6. Masti Gate
      7. Mochi Gate
      8. Mori Gate
      9. Roshnai Gate
      10. Shahalmi Gate
      11. Sheranwala Gate
      12. Taxali Gate
      13. Yakki Gate

      After the elections of 1988, who became the Prime Minister of Pakistan?

      Benazir Bhutto

      • Born: June 21, 1953, in Karachi.
      • Assassinated: December 27, 2007, in Rawalpindi.
      • Spouse: Asif Ali Zardari (1987–2007).
      • Party: Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP).
      • Children: Bilawal Bhutto Zardari, Bakhtawar Bhutto Zardari, Aseefa Bhutto Zardari.
      • Education: Lady Margaret Hall (1973–1977).

      Political Career:

      • Sworn in as First Woman Prime Minister of Pakistan on December 2, 1988.
      • First term as Prime Minister: 1988 to 1990.
      • Second term as Prime Minister: 1993 to 1996.
      • Farooq Laghari dissolved her second government.
      • Served as the 11th and 13th Prime Minister of Pakistan.

      • Nursery School: Lady Jennings Nursery School in Karachi.

      Name the Prime Minister who presented Objectives Resolution in 1949?

      Correct Answer: Liaquat Ali Khan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Constituent Assembly Pakistan adopted the Objectives Resolution on March 12, 1949.

      Prime Minister, Liaquat Ali Khan, had presented it in the assembly on March 7, 1949

      Out of 75 members of the assembly, 21 voted for it.

      All the amendments proposed by minority members were reject

      Consequently, all ten of them voted against it.

      The resolution, in its entirety, has been made part of the Constitution of Pakistan under Article 2(A).

      *********



      106

      Who was the leader of Quit India Movement?

      Correct Answer: Mahatma Gandhi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Quit India Movement is also known as the August Movement.

      It was launched by Gandhi on August 8, 1942, in Bombay.

      The movement, started by the Congress party.

      It's demanded an end to British rule in India.

      Muhammad Ali Jinnah responded with the slogan "Divide and Quit India."

      107

      In Pakistan legislative powers are vested in the ______?

      Correct Answer: Parliament
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • In Pakistan, legislative powers are exercised by the Parliament.
    • It  consists of the National Assembly and the Senate.
    • The Parliament is responsible for making, amending, and repealing laws under the Constitution of Pakistan.

      Correct Answer: Bolan River
      Show explanation

    • Discovered in 1974 by French archaeologists Jean-François Jarrige and Catherine Jarrige.
      • 109

      The government has renamed the Micro Finance Bank as ______?

      Correct Answer: Khushali Bank
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • The government renamed the Micro Finance Bank as Khushali Bank.
    • Founded in 2000, it supports low-income groups with microfinance services.
      • 110

      Marines fight on ______?

      Correct Answer: Sea
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Marines specialize in amphibious operations.

      It fighting on sea and transitioning to land during combat missions.

      111

      What is the total strength of members in the National Security Council (Pakistan)?

      Correct Answer: 11
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The total strength of members in the National Security Council (Pakistan) is 11.

      The NSC was established in 1999 by Pervez Musharraf.

      112

      Which of the following made newest division of Punjab on 17 August 2022?

      Correct Answer: Gujrat
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • List of 10 Divisions of Punjab
      • 1.       Bahawalpur
      • 2.       Dera Ghazi Khan
      • 3.       Faisalabad
      • 4.       Gujranwala
      • 5.       Gujrat
      • 6.       Lahore
      • 7.       Multan
      • 8.       Rawalpindi
      • 9.       Sahiwal
      • 10.     Sargodha
      • Gujrat given status of 10th division of Punjab on 17 August 2022.
      • Gujrat division consists of Gujrat, Mandi Bahauddin and Hafiz Abad districts.
      113

      The International Cricket Council (ICC) independent tribunal, which recently banned three Pakistani cricketers for spot-fixing, held hearings at ______?

      Correct Answer: Doha
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • The ICC banned three Pakistan players—Salman Butt, Mohammad Asif, and Mohammad Amir—for 5 to 10 years over spot-fixing.
    • In November 2011, Butt and Asif were convicted by a London court on criminal charges related to spot-fixing.
      • 114

      Who was the First Governor of former NWFP at the time of independence of Pakistan in 1947?

      Correct Answer: Sir George Cunningham
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      First Governors of Provinces of Pakistan:

      • KPK: Sir George Cunningham (British).
      • Sindh: Ghulam Hussain Hidayatullah - 14 August 1947 to 4 October 1948 (Pakistani)
      • Punjab: Sir Francis Mudie (British).
      • Balochistan: Sir Geoffrey Prior (British).
      115

      The statutory body mandated to act as a national focal point on all police-related matters is ______?

      Correct Answer: National Police Bureau
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The National Police Bureau (NPB) is the statutory body in Pakistan that acts as the national focal point for police-related matters.

      The NPB works with all the federal and provincial criminal investigation agencies in Pakistan.

      The NPB also collaborates with the National Public Safety Commission (NPSC).

      116

      Inspector-General of Police is also known as ______?

      Correct Answer: Provincial Police Officer
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Inspector General of Police (IGP) is also known as the Provincial Police Officer (PPO).

      The IGP is the head of the police force in a province.

      It is appointed by the federal government with the consent of the provincial chief minister.

      The IGP is a Police Service of Pakistan officer who has administrative and financial powers. 

      117

      The fate of Khyber-Pakhtunkhwa (former NWFP) at the time of independence was decided by _____?

      Correct Answer: Referendum
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • The North-West Frontier Province referendum was held in July 1947 before the Partition of India.
    • The people of NWFP voted in favor of joining Pakistan.
      • 118

      In the National assembly of Pakistan, minorities are allocated ______ seats?

      Correct Answer: Ten
      Show explanation

      ProvinceGeneralWomenNon-MuslimsTotal
      Punjab1413310174
      Sindh611475
      KPK45954
      Balochistan16420
      Islamabad3-3
       2666010336
      119

      Which district of KPK registered zero turnout of women voters in General Elections in February 2024?

      Correct Answer: Buner
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      As per analysis of election data carried out by Pattan-Coalition 38, at least four constituencies in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa including Buner saw zero turnout of women voters across several polling stations.

      General elections in Pakistan held on 8 February 2024.

      120

      یادوں کی بارات کس نے لکھی ہے؟

      Year of publication: 1970

      Language: Urdu

      Subjects: Autobiography

      ND14-3-2023

      121

      Sher-e-Bengal was the title of _____?

      Correct Answer: A.K Fazal-Ul-Haq
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      He was serving as the first and longest Prime Minister of Bengal during the British Raj.

      He presenting the Lahore Resolution in 1940.

      He was died in Dhaka on April 27, 1962.

      122

      The temporary or permanent release of a prisoner before the expiry of a sentence, on the promise of good behavior is called ______?

      Correct Answer: Parole
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Parole means کسی قیدی کو عارضی رہائی دینا

      Example: He has been released on parole.

      123

      Reko Diq is famous for reserves of ____?

      Correct Answer: Gold and copper
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Reko Diq represents one of the largest copper and gold reserves in the world.

      The Reko Diq Mine is the world's 5th largest gold deposit.

      The Reko Diq Mine is a planned mining operation, located near Reko Diq town in Chagai District, Baluchistan, Pakistan

      124

      A person appointed as Chief Election Commissioner of Pakistan shall hold office for a term of ______?

      Correct Answer: 5 Years
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The President of Pakistan appoint the Chief Election Commissioner and four members of Election Commission of Pakistan.

      Election commission was established in 1950.

      125

      The Shimla agreement was signed between Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto and _______?

      Correct Answer: Indira Gandhi
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • The Shimla Agreement was signed on 2 July 1972 
    • Signed between Indian Prime Minister Indira Gandhi and Pakistani President Z. A. Bhutto.
    • Context : Indo-Pakistani War of 1971
    • Location: Barnes Court (Raj Bhavan), Shimla, Himachal Pradesh, India
    • Sealed: 7 August 1972
    • Ratified: 15 July 1972 (by Pakistan), 3 August 1972 (by India)
    • Effective: 4 August 1972
    • Last updated by NA on 23-April-2026
      • 126

      Who suspended the 1973 Constitution in 1977?

      Correct Answer: General Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      General Zia-ul-Haq suspended the 1973 Constitution in 1977 after taking power through a military coup.


      On July 5, 1977. Gen Zia removes Bhutto, suspending the constitution and declaring martial law


      Additional information:

      The National Assembly approved the 1973 Constitution on April 10, 1973

      • and it came into effect on August 14, 1973.

      • Zulfikar Ali Bhutto was the Prime Minister of Pakistan from this date

      • and Fazal Ilahi Chaudhry was appointed as the President of Pakistan.


      This Constitution is called the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan 1973


      127

      Qissa khawani Bazar is in which district of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Peshawar
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Qissa Khwani Bazaar is a bazaar in Peshawar, KPK, Pakistan.

      It is one of the oldest place in Peshawar city.

      It is now one of the busiest places in Peshawar. 

      128

      Who was the ruler of Kashmir at the time of partition?

      Correct Answer: Hari Singh
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Maharaja Sir Hari Singh was the last ruling Maharaja of the princely state of Jammu and Kashmir. 

      In 1947 Hari Singh, who had ascended the throne of Kashmir in 1925

      Reign: 23 September 1925 — 17 November 1952

      Predecessor: Pratap Singh

      Born: September 23, 1895, Jammu

      Died: April 26, 1961, Mumbai, India


      ***

      The first Muslim ruler of Kashmir was Shah Mir

      129

      Mr. Raza Rabbani served as the_____?

      Correct Answer: Chairman Senate
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Mian Raza Rabbani is a Pakistani politician who served as the 7th Chairman of the Senate of Pakistan from March 2015 to March 2018.

      130

      The Elite Force Punjab was formed in _______?

      Correct Answer: 1998
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      The Elite Force of Punjab was formed in 1998 by then Punjab Chief Minister Shahbaz Sharif.
      The Elite Force is a special force that assists district police with high-risk searches, raids, and rescue operations.
      131

      Khuzdar and Kalat districts are in the Province of _____?

      Correct Answer: Balochistan
      Show explanation
      Explanation
       Khuzdar and Kalat districts are in the Province of Balochistan, Pakistan. 
      Kalat Division is an administrative division of Balochistan Province of Pakistan.
      Its capital city is Khuzdar,
      Founded on 14 October 1955.
      Founded by the Khanate of Kalat
      CNIC Code of Kalat Division is 51.
      According to the 2023 Pakistani census population of Kalat Division is 2,719,964 (2.7 million).
      132

      The Punjab Police Department consists of how many Regional Offices?

      Correct Answer: 10
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Punjab Police Department in Pakistan has 10 Regional Police Offices (RPOs):

      1. Bahawalpur
      2. D.G. Khan
      3. Faisalabad
      4. Gujranwala
      5. Lahore
      6. Multan
      7. Rawalpindi
      8. Sahiwal
      9. Sargodha
      10. Sheikhupura

      The Punjab Police is the largest police force in Pakistan, with over 179,000 personnel.

      133

      Council of Common Interests (CCI) is a constitutional body of Pakistan. Membership of CCI consists of:

      Correct Answer: Prime Minister, all Provincial Chief Ministers and 3 members nominated by the Prime Minister
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      CCI stands for Council of Common Interests.

      It is a constitutional body set up under Article 153 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973.

      Membership of the current CCI consists of ...

      • All four Provincial Chief Ministers
      • Three members to be nominated by the Prime Minister (Usually Cabinet members).
      134

      The writer of National anthem Hafeez Jalandhari died?

      Correct Answer: 1982
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hafeez Jalandhari: Known for writing the national anthem of Pakistan, "Qaumi Taranah."

       National Anthem of Pakistan:Written by: Hafeez Jalandhari in 1952

      Born: 14 January 1900Jalandhar

      Died: 21 December 1982 (aged 82)Lahore

      Pen name: Abu Al-Asar

      He studied in a mosque and then in a local school to 7th grade.

      Notable awards

      • Pride of Performance in 1958
      • Hilal-e-Imtiaz
      135

      India clam with British Crown with

      Correct Answer: Introduction of government of India Act
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The British Crown assumed direct control over India in 1858, following the Indian Rebellion of 1857.

      It is also known as the Sepoy Mutiny.

      This marked the end of the East India Company's rule and the beginning of the British Raj, which lasted until India gained independence in 1947.

      The British ruled India directly for 90 years, from 1858 to 1947.

      136

      Congress accepted the Partition Plan immediately but Muslim League could not accept it immediately. at that time Jinnah opined that:

      Correct Answer: Plan would be submitted to Muslim League council for discussion
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Jinnah emphasized that the Muslim League needed to carefully review the Partition Plan before making a final decision,

      Showing their hesitation to accept it without considering its implications for Muslims in India.

      137

      Which country has announced to give Rs 2.3 billion to Pakistan for weather installation in Multan?

      Correct Answer: Japan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Government of Japan has announced that it will provide 2.8 billion Japanese Yen (equivalent to around 20.57 million USD) for the installation of a weather surveillance radar in Multan, Pakistan.

      The total project cost is estimated at PKR 6188.759 million, with the Government of Pakistan contributing PKR 600.055 million

      138

      What is the current literacy rate in Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: 59.13%
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      According to the latest survey conducted from 2022 to 2024, Pakistan’s literacy rate stands at an encouraging 59.13%, maintaining a stable trajectory.

      The primary level has played a pivotal role in boosting the overall literacy rate, witnessing a jump from 53% to 57%.

      Similarly, women’s literacy rates have also seen a positive increase, climbing from 54% to 57%.

      Notably, male literacy has shown significant growth over the past three years.

      To put things into perspective, there were 22,650,000 admissions at the primary level, 2,884,400 at the secondary level, and 194,900 at the college level.


      139

      In the 18th Amendment, in which year was the responsibility for textbook development shifted from Federal to Provincial?

      Correct Answer: 2010
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The 18th Amendment was passed in the National Assembly on April 8, 2010 and received the assent of the President on April 19, 2010.

      The amendment devolved the responsibility for education policy, planning, curriculum, and standards to the provinces.

      140

      اقبال کا قیام یورپ کا راستہ کون سا ہے

      Correct Answer: 1905 – 1908
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      علامہ اقبال نے 1905 سے 1908 کے دوران یورپ کا قیام کیا۔

      اس دوران انہوں نے کیمرج یونیورسٹی سے فلسفہ میں ڈگری حاصل کی،

      لنکنز اِن سے وکالت کی تربیت مکمل کی،

      اور جرمنی کی ہائیڈل برگ یونیورسٹی سے فلسفے میں ڈاکٹریٹ کی ڈگری حاصل کی۔ یورپ کے اس قیام نے ان کے فکری اور فلسفیانہ نظریات پر گہرا اثر ڈالا۔

      141

      Muhammad Khan Achakzai was the Governor of _____?

      Correct Answer: Balochistan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Muhammad Khan Achakzai served as the 20th Governor of Balochistan.

      He served from 14 June 2013 to 9 October 2018.

      142

      Which article of the 1973 constitution provides hat no person shall be deprived of life or liberty save in accordance with law?

      Correct Answer: 9
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Article 9 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan states, "No person shall be deprived of life or liberty save in accordance with law"

      The 24th Amendment reallocated National Assembly seats among federating units.

      It allowed election authorities to update constituency boundaries based on the provisional results of the 2017 Census.
      143

      The largest copper deposits in Balochistan are found in the area of?

      Correct Answer: Chaghi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Reko Diq represents one of the largest copper and gold reserves in the world 

      The Reko Diq Mine is a planned mining operation, located near Reko Diq town in Chagai District

      The biggest deposit of copper is present in Reko Diq, discovered in 1969.

      Tethyan is seeking to develop the Reko Diq copper-gold deposit in the Chagai Hills

      Reko Diq is a town in Balochistan.


      144

      In 1997, a senior police officer, Muhammad Ashraf Marath, embraced Shahadat in a terrorist attack. Where did it take place?

      Correct Answer: Gujranwala
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • In 1997, senior police officer Muhammad Ashraf Marath was martyred in a terrorist attack in Gujranwala.
    • His sacrifice is remembered as a symbol of bravery and dedication to duty.
      • 145

      Which of the following Princely States was the first to formally accede to Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Bahawalpur
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Out of 565 princely states during 1947, 13 princely states decided to join Pakistan. These 13 princely states are:

          Bahawalpur: On 3 October 1947, the Nawab of Bahawalpur, Sadeq Mohammad Khan V, acceded his state to Pakistan, becoming the first ruler to do so successfully.

          Khairpur: The state of Khairpur acceded to Pakistan on 3 October 1947.

          ***

          Chitral: The Mehtar of Chitral, Muzaffar-ul-Mulk, stated his intention to accede to Pakistan on 15 August 1947.

          On 28 July 1969, President Yahya Khan announced the full integration

          ***

            The largest Princely state by area at the time of partition was Kashmir.

            ND29-5-2023


            146

            Which the viceroy had the longest tenure in India?

            Correct Answer: Linlithgow
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • Lord Linlithgow served as Viceroy of India from 1936 to 1944, the longest tenure of 8 years.
          • Lord Mountbatten had the shortest tenure, serving from March 22 to August 15, 1947.
          • Lord Canning was the first viceroy of British India.
          • Warren Hastings was the first governor-general of india.
            • 147

            Which US president visited Pakistan first?

            Correct Answer: Dwight D Eisenhower
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Five Presidents of USA have visited Pakistan.

            They are following:

            NameLocaleRemarksDate
            Dwight D. EisenhowerKarachiInformal visit; met with President Ayub Khan.December 7–9, 1959
            Lyndon B. JohnsonKarachiMet with President Ayub Khan.December 23, 1967
            Richard M. NixonLahoreState visit; met with President Yahya Khan.August 1–2, 1969
            William J. ClintonIslamabadMet with President Tarar and General Musharraf; delivered radio address.March 25, 2000
            George W. BushIslamabadMet with President Musharraf.March 3–4, 2006
            148

            Name the ancient site that was the forerunner of the Indus Civilization?

            Correct Answer: Kot Diji
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Kot Diji was the forerunner of the Indus Civilization. 

            Kot Diji Fort was built around 1790 by the Talpur dynasty ruler of Upper Sindh, Mir Suhrab, who reigned from 1783 to 1830 AD.

            This fort built on the ridge of a steep narrow hill is well-preserved.

            149

            Durand Line Treaty was signed between Sir Mortimer Durand and King ______ of Afghanistan in 1893.

            Correct Answer: Abdul Rehman
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • The Pak-Afghan border, known as the Durand Line, was drawn in 1893.
            • It was established through an agreement between the British Indian government and the Afghan Amir, Abdur Rahman Khan.
            • The line demarcates the boundary between Pakistan and Afghanistan.
            150

            The first female scuba diving instructor of Pakistan is _____?

            Correct Answer: Rosheen Khan
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • Rosheen Khan is Pakistan's first female scuba diving instructor and the only Nitrox diving instructor.
          • She is also known as the "Mermaid of Churna Island."
          • Yousuf Ali mentored her at the Karachi Scuba Diving Center.
            • 151

            Nai Gaj Dam is being constructed in which province of Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Sindh
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Nai Gaj Dam is being constructed in the Dadu District of Sindh Province, Pakistan 

            It is scheduled to be completed by the end of 2024.

            152

            'Naryab Dam' is being constructed in the district of _____?

            Correct Answer: Hangu
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • Naryab Dam is located in Tehsil Thal, Hangu District, Khyber-Pakhtunkhwa.
          • It is a small reservoir providing water for irrigation and drinking during droughts.
          • No other dam exists within a 55 km² area.
          • Designed by Mussadiq Khan Bangash, an electrical electronics engineer.
            • 153

            1937 election were held in eleven provinces of India Sub Continent according to?

            Correct Answer: Act of 1935
            Show explanation
            Explanation

             Provincial elections were held in British India in the winter of 1936–37 as mandated by the Government of India Act 1935

            Elections were held in eleven provinces -


            1. Madras
            2. Central Provinces
            3. Bihar
            4. Orissa
            5. United Provinces
            6. Bombay Presidency
            7. Assam
            8. NWFP
            9. Bengal
            10. Punjab
            11. Sindh
            154

            Who is known as he father of Civil Service in India?

            Correct Answer: Charles Cornwallis
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Charles Cornwallis is often referred to as the 'Father of Civil Service in India'.


            Cornwallis was the Governor-General of India from 1786 to 1793 and again from 1805 to 1806. 

            155

            The Trpartite Agreement of 9 April 1974 was singed bewteen Pakistan India and Bangladesh who signed he document on behalf of Pakistan?ZA butto, Agha Shani, Sharif-ud-Din Peerzada, Aziz Ahmed

            Correct Answer: Aziz Ahmed
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            On behalf of Pakistan, Aziz Ahmed signed the Tripartite Agreement 

            On behalf of India, it was signed by Sardar Swaran Singh

            On behalf of Bangladesh, it was signed by Dr. Kamal Hossain,

            ***

            The Tripartite Agreement of 9 April 1974

            It was signed between Pakistan, India, and Bangladesh 

            To address the issue of the prisoners of war and other related matters following the 1971 war.


            156

            Who was appointed as caretaker Prime Minister on March 25, 2013?

            Correct Answer: Mir Hazar Khan Khoso
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Mir Hazar Khan Khoso. was a Pakistani jurist

            He was the caretaker prime minister of Pakistan from 25 March to 5 June 2013.

            157

            When a devastating Earthquake joined KPK Former (NWFP) and Kashmir killing thousand of people?

            Correct Answer: Oct 08 2005
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The devastating earthquake that affected KPK and Kashmir occurred on October 8, 2005.

            This earthquake, known as the 2005 Kashmir earthquake, had a magnitude of 7.6

            158

            اختر حسین رائے پوری کا تعلق کسی ادبی تحریک سے تھا؟

            Correct Answer: ترقی پسند تحریک
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            اختر حسین رائے پوری کا تعلق ترقی پسند تحریک سے تھا۔

            وہ ترقی پسند ادب کے بانیوں میں شمار ہوتے ہیں اور ان کے نظریات نے اردو ادب پر گہرا اثر چھوڑا۔

            159

            Pakistan lies of the tropic zone of _____?

            Correct Answer: Tropic of Cancer
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Pakistan lies in the tropical zone of the Tropic of Cancer.
            • It is an imaginary line that circles the Earth at approximately 23.5° north latitude.
            • Pakistan's geographical location between 24° and 37° north latitude places it within the Tropic of Cancer zone.
            160

            Who was the First Governor of Sindh after independence?

            Correct Answer: Ghulam Hussain Hidayatullah
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            First Governors of Provinces of Pakistan:

            • Sindh: Ghulam Hussain Hidayatullah - 14 August 1947 to 4 October 1948 (Pakistani)
            • Punjab: Sir Francis Mudie (British).
            • Balochistan: Sir Geoffrey Prior (British).
            • KPK: Sir George Cunningham (British).
            161

            According to Shah Waliullah, the biggest cause of the plight of Muslims at that time was _____?

            Correct Answer: Moral degradation
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • Shah Waliullah believed that moral degradation was the primary reason for the decline of Muslims.
          • He emphasizing a need for ethical and spiritual reform.
            • 162

            According to Article 44 of the Constitution, the President of Pakistan shall hold office for a term of _____?

            Correct Answer: 5 years
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Under Article 44 of the 1973 Constitution, the President's term is five years.

            The President of Pakistan is the Head of State.

            If the President is absent, the Chairman of the Senate of Pakistan acts as the President.

            163

            Which Pakistani player scored the fastest first 1000 ODI runs (in 21 innings)?

            Correct Answer: Babar Azam
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • Babar Azam scored the fastest first 1000 ODI runs by a Pakistani player in just 21 innings.
          • Fastest 1000 T20I runs (26 innings): Mohammad Rizwan.
          • Fastest 1000 Test runs (17 innings): Azhar Mahmood.
            • 164

            Pakistan and Afghanistan signed a 'Transit Trade Agreement' in which year?

            Correct Answer: 1965
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • Pakistan and Afghanistan signed the Transit Trade Agreement (ATTA) on 2nd March 1965.
          • Pakistan and Afghanistan signed the Afghanistan–Pakistan Transit Trade Agreement (APTTA) in 2010.
          • It replaced the 1965 Afghanistan Transit Trade Agreement (ATTA).
          • The APTTA was enforced on June 12, 2011.
          • On 23 October 2017 the President of Afghanistan Ashraf Ghani announced that "APTTA has expired".
            • 165

            Nasir-ud-Din Qabacha was a lieutenant of Muhammad Ghauri. He also served as Governor of _____?

            Correct Answer: Multan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Nasir-ud-Din Qabacha or Kaba-cha was the Muslim Turkic governor of Multan.

            He was appointed by Sultan Shahabuddin Muhammad Ghauri in 1203.

            166

            Who was the first Muslim President of Indian National Congress?

            Correct Answer: Badruddin Tyabji
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Badruddin Tyabji was the first Muslim President of the Indian National Congress
            • Elected during the third session in Madras in 1887
            • Born: 10 October 1844, Bombay
            • Died, 19 August 1906 (aged 61), London
            167

            According to the Police Order 2002, the head of Police in a District is ______?

            Correct Answer: District Police Officer
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Police Order 2002 is a law that governs the police force in Pakistan.

            According to this law, the District Police Officer (DPO) is the head of the police in a district, responsible for maintaining law and order.

            168

            What was the old name of Lahore High Court?

            Correct Answer: Chief court of punjab
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Established on 21 March 1919.

            Number of position 60.

            Judge term length 62 years.

            There are 5 high courts in Pakistan.

            169

            The main reason of Partition of Bengal was _____?

            Correct Answer: Administrative requirements
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The main reason for the Partition of Bengal in 1905 was to improve administrative efficiency.

            Partition of Bengal was annulled by Lord Hardinge 

            It was officially announced on 19 July 1905

            This decision was made in response to widespread protests and riots from the Swadeshi movement, which opposed the 1905 partition.

            The partition of Bengal was canceled by Lord Hardinge II.

            170

            The Objectives Resolution was made part of substantive provisions of the Constitution of 1973 ______?

            Correct Answer: By Presidential Order 14 of 1985 (Revival of Constitution Order)
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Objectives Resolution was made a substantive part of the Constitution of 1973 through Presidential Order 14 of 1985 during General Zia-ul-Haq's regime.

            It is also known as the Revival of Constitution Order.

            The order made the Objectives Resolution an integral part of the Constitution through Article 2A of the Eighth Amendment.

            The order took effect on March 2, 1985.

            171

            Jamia Millia Islamia was established at ______?

            Correct Answer: Aligarh
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Jamia Millia Islamia, an institution originally established at Aligarh in United Provinces, India in 1920.

            It became a Central University by an act of the Indian Parliament in 1988.

            172

            Where did Sir Aga Khan meet which congress leader in 1906?

            Correct Answer: Viceroy's Lodge in Simla
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            In 1906, Sir Aga Khan met with a prominent Congress leader at the Viceregal Lodge in Shimla


            The Simla Deputation was a gathering of 35 prominent Indian Muslim leaders


            The             Aga Khan III led them at the Viceregal Lodge in Simla in October 1906.


            The deputation aimed to convince Lord Minto, then Viceroy of India, to grant Muslims greater representation in politics.


            173

            "Abhi to main jawan hoon" was written by ______?

            Correct Answer: Hafeez Jalandhari
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            "Abhi Toh Main Jawan Hoon" was written by the Pakistani poet Hafeez Jalandhari.

            The poem was originally sung by Malika Pukhraj.

            There is also a song called "Abhi Toh Main Jawan Hoon" by Alisha Chinai from the 2006 album The Killer.

            The song was composed by Sajid-Wajid.

            174

            The first flight of the Shaheen in 1999 was fired from the _____ Base.

            Correct Answer: Sonmiani Naval
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • The Shaheen-I missile was test-fired on 15 April 1999 from the Sonmiani Test Range.
          • The test was conducted by a joint team of NDC and SRC.
          • It was led by Dr. Samar Mubarakmand.

            All about Zia-ul-Haq

            Zia-Ul-Haq Died on 17-08-1988 by Plane crashed in Bahawalpur

            American Ambassador Arnold Raphael with President Zia Ul Haque in a plane crash

            General Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq HI, GCSJ, ร.ม.ภ, was a Pakistani four-star general 

            Muhammad Zia-Ul-Haq became the CMLA of Pakistan in: 1977

            Born: August 12, 1924, Jalandhar, India

            Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq took oath as President of Pakistan on 23 March 1985.

            Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq becomes 6th President of Pakistan.

            General Zia Ul Haq ruled Pakistan from 1977 to 1988

            The provincial constitution order martial law in 1977.

            When PCO was introduced by Zia ul Haq? 1981

            When General Zia Ul Haq addressed in UN? 23 Oct 1985

            He remains the country's longest-serving de facto head of state and Chief of Army Staff

            Ghulam Ishaq Khan succeeded Zia Ul Haque as President of Pakistan.

            In the Era of Zia ul Haq who was the Prime Minister of Pakistan: Muhammad Khan Junejo

            177

            Pakistan conducted its nuclear explosion in Raskoh Chagai and Mekran Coast on ____?

            Correct Answer: 28 May 1998
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pakistan conducted a series of nuclear tests on 28 May 1998 at the Chagai test site, in Balochistan.

            5 Nuclear tests were conducted by Pakistan at 15:15 hrs PST on 28 May 1998.

            The tests were performed at Ras Koh Hills in the Chagai District of Balochistan Province.

            Chagai-I was Pakistan's first public test of nuclear weapons.

            while 

            Chagai II

            Chagai-II is the codename assigned to the second atomic test conducted by Pakistan, carried out on 30 May 1998

            These tests followed the Chagai-I series

            The test site was Kharan Desert, Pakistan

            The number of tests is 1

            178

            Which conference was held from 27th November 1947 to 1st December 1947 in Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Education
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The first Educational Conference in Pakistan, held from 27th November 1947 to 1st December 1947, was headed by Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah.

            179

            Kashmir Independence movement started in which year?

            Correct Answer: 1931
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The modern Kashmir independence movement began on July 13, 1931.

            The Government of Azad Jammu and Kashmir was established on October 24, 1947.

            180

            The Lawyers' Movement, also known as the Movement for the Restoration of Judiciary, was actually the restoration of ____?

            Correct Answer: Iftikhar Chaudhry
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The movement was led by SCBA presidents:

            Iftikhar M. Chaudhry

            Munir A. Malik

            Aitzaz Ahsan

            Ali Ahmad Kurd

            Nawaz Sharif

            Latif Afridi

            ****

            The Lawyers' Movement, also known as the Movement for the Restoration of Judiciary or the Black Coat Protests,

            The Lawyers' Movement was the popular mass protest movement initiated by the lawyers of Pakistan in response to the former president and army chief Pervez Musharraf's actions of 9 March 2007

            Date: 9 March 2007 — 17 March 2009

            Location: Nationwide, throughout Pakistan

            Caused by: Suspension of chief justice Iftikhar Muhammad Chaudhry

            ND-18-8-2023


            181

            Which dam supplies drinking water to Islamabad and Rawalpindi?

            Correct Answer: Khanpur dam
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Khanpur Dam is located on the Haro River in Pakistan.

            The dam is situated near the city of Khanpur, in the province of Punjab.

            It was completed in 1983.

            The dam is used for irrigation, water supply, and hydroelectric power generation.

            182

            Which province passed the resolution in 1938 for Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Sindh
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Sindh Provincial Muslim League had passed a resolution on 10 October, 1938.

            The Sindh Legislative Assembly followed suit and passed a resolution in support of Pakistan on March 3, 1943.

            G. M. Syed, an influential Sindhi activist, revolutionary, and Sufi 

            later G. M. Syed is one of the important leaders at the forefront of the Sindh independence movement,

            G. M. Syed joined the Muslim League in 1938 

            G. M. Syed presented the Pakistan resolution in the Sindh Assembly.

            ND14-7-2023

            183

            Iftikhar Muhammad Chaudhry was restored as Chief Justice by the Supreme Court on _____?

            Correct Answer: 20 July 2007
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            On 9 March 2007, Musharraf asked Chaudhry to resign, which he refused to do, and was suspended from office.

            The resultant civil disorder led to the Lawyers' Movement, which succeeded in the restoration of Chaudhry as Chief Justice on 20 July 2007.


            184

            "Taleem-e-Balighan" drama was written by _____?

            Correct Answer: Khawaja Moinuddin
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Taleem-e-Balighan is a 1956 Pakistani social satire TV serial which first aired on PTV in 1966.

            It was written by Khawaja Moinuddin.

            It is considered one of the classics of Pakistani television by some TV critics.


            185

            Which province of Pakistan has the lowest population density?

            Correct Answer: Balochistan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Least populated Official province of Pakistan Is Balochistan.

            Its density is low due to the mountainous terrain and scarcity of water. 

            Balochistan is the largest province of Pakistan by land area

            186

            Asar-ul-Sanadeed was compiled by ___?

            Correct Answer: Sir Syed Ahmad Khan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Asar-ul-Sanadeed, a detailed historical and architectural account of Delhi.

            It was compiled by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan in 1847.

            It showcasing his interest in history and culture.

            187

            Who drafted the constitution of 1973 in Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Zulifqar Ali Bhutto
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Constitution of 1973:

            Zulifqar Ali Bhutto introduced new constitution in Pakistan on August 14, 1973.

            According to 1973 constitution, both national assembly and senate elect president for Pakistan whereas the prime minister is selected by national assembly.

            Bicameral Legislature system was introduced for the first time in Pakistan in the constitution of 1973.

            According to this constitution, a candidate must be 25 to contest for the election of national assembly.


            188

            Who is Suhail Sarwar Warraich?

            Correct Answer: Journalist
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • Suhail Warraich is a prominent Pakistani journalist, columnist, and television host known for his analytical style.
          • He is famous for hosting the political talk show "Ek Din Geo Ke Saath".
          • He was born on 8 November 1961.
            • 189

            The Liaquat Ali Khan was assassinated in which city?

            Correct Answer: Rawalpindi
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            On 16 October 1951, Liaquat Ali Khan was shot twice in the chest while addressing a gathering in Rawalpindi.

            This attack was linked to the Rawalpindi Conspiracy, an attempted coup d'état against his government.

            The case hearing began on 14 June 1951 in Hyderabad Jail.

            It involving figures like Faiz Ahmad Faiz and Major General Akbar Khan.

            • Asrar-e- Khudi was the first philosophical poetry book of Allama Iqbal.

             Its foundation stone was laid by the then Prime Minister of Pakistan in 1998

            Harappa is an archeological site belonging to which civilization?

            Correct Answer: Indus Valley Civilization
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Harappa was an Indus civilization urban centre.

            It lies in Punjab Province, Pakistan, on an old bank/bed of the River Ravi. 

            Harappa was the first site of the civilization to be excavated in 1921.

            The excavation team was led by Daya Ram Sahni.

            Show explanation

            Construction of Badshahi Mosque in Lahore completed in:1673

            In 1947, Pakistan uplifted the Badshahi Mosque and citadel Lahore as symbols of the state.

            Gulab Singh was subsequently bestowed with the title of Maharaja.

            Which one of the following is the biggest man made lake found in Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Keenjhar lake
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            It is commonly called Malik Lake.

            It is situated about 36 kilometres from the city of Thatta.

            It is the largest fresh water lake in Pakistan.

            Area: 134.7 km²

            Length: 24 km

            Width: 6 km

            ***

            Mirani Dam is in Kech District in the Balochistan province of Pakistan.


            Mirani Dam was completed in July 2006 and began impounding the Dasht River in August 2006.


            The feasibility report of the Mirani dam project was first completed in 1956. 


            Mirani Dam was commissioned by(WAPDA) in 2001 to provide water for Gwadar city.
