            Boundary agreement was signed between Pakistan and China in?

            Correct Answer: 2 March 1963
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            A boundary agreement was signed between Pakistan and China in 2 March 1963.

            Negotiations between the nations officially began on October 13, 1962.

            It was signed by:

            • Foreign Minister Chen Yi for the Chinese
            • Zulfikar Ali Bhutto for the Pakistani

            The eastern tip of the Wakhan Corridor starts the Sino-Pak border between the People's Republic of China and Pakistan.

            206

            National Command and Operation Center (NCOC) was formed in _____?

            Correct Answer: April 2020
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            NCOC was formed in April 2020 in response to the COVID-19 pandemic.

            Prime Minister Imran Khan ordered its establishment to centralize information from all provinces and territories.

            207

            The capital of British India was transferred from Calcutta to ______ in the year 1911.

            Correct Answer: Delhi
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The capital of British India was transferred from Calcutta (now Kolkata) to Delhi in the year 1911.

            The announcement to shift the capital was made during the Delhi Durbar  held in December 1911 to commemorate the coronation of King George V.


            The new capital in Delhi was known as New Delhi and was designed by the architect Edwin Lutyens.

            It became the center of British colonial administration in India.

            208

            Pakistan left CENTO in which year?

            Correct Answer: 1979
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Middle East Treaty Organization, also known as the Central Treaty Organization

            CENTO was a military alliance of the Cold War.

            Headquarters: Baghdad (1955 – 1958) Ankara (1958 – 1979)

            Abbreviation: METO

            CENTO was formed on 24 February 1955 by:

            • Iran
            • Iraq
            • Pakistan
            • Turkey
            • and the United Kingdom.

            CENTO alliance was dissolved on 16 March 1979

            related question

            Both Iran and Pakistan remained members of CENTO till?

            1979
            Pakistan left CENTO in which year____?
            1979

            When Pakistan signed the Baghdad pact, later called CENTO:
            23 September 1955

            Fatima Surraya Bajiya is a renowned _____?

            Correct Answer: Novelist and playwright

            Explanation

            • She is widely regarded as one of the most important voices in contemporary Urdu literature.
            210

            پاکستان معدنی دولت سے پوری طرح فائدہ نہ اٹھانے کی کیا وجہ ہے

            Correct Answer: سب جواب درست ہیں

             ان تمام وجوہات کی بنا پر پاکستان اپنی معدنی دولت سے بھرپور استفادہ نہیں کر سکا۔


            211

            علامہ اقبال نے کس ملک کی تعلیمی پالیسی مرتب کی تھی؟

            Correct Answer: افغانستان
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            علامہ اقبال نے افغانستان کی تعلیمی پالیسی مرتب کرنے میں اہم کردار ادا کیا تھا۔

            وہ افغانستان کے تعلیمی نظام میں اصلاحات کی حمایت کرتے تھے اور انہوں نے وہاں کے حکام کو جدید تعلیم کی اہمیت پر زور دیا تھا۔







            212

            قائد اعظم گورنر جنرل کی حیثیت سے کتنی تنخواہ لیتے تھے؟

            Correct Answer: ایک روپیہ
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            یہ ان کی سادگی اور قوم کے لیے قربانی کی علامت تھی۔






            213

            What was the role of the Quaid-i-Azam for enacting the Rowlatt Act?

            Correct Answer: Opposed it
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Quaid-i-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah opposed the Rowlatt Act of 1919

            He resigned from the Imperial Legislative Council as a protest against Rowlatt Act. 

            The Chaotic and Revolutionary Crimes Act of 1919, commonly known as the Rowlatt Act,

            It was the act of the Imperial Legislative Council on March 18, 1919, in Delhi.

            214

            The Book “Higher Than Hope: The Biography Of Nelson Mandela” is written by?

            Correct Answer: Fatima Meer
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            It was published in 1990.

            215

            Silk Route is another name for?

            Correct Answer: Korakoram Highway
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Silk Route is also known as Karakoram Highway

            The silk road connects Pakistan and China.

            The total length is approximately 1,300 kilometers, of which 887 kilometers are in Pakistan and 413 km in China.

            The silk road is considered a symbol of Pakistan-China's eternal friendship

            The highway meets the Pakistani-Chinese border at Khunjerab Pass

             It was started in 1959 and was completed and opened to the public in 1979.

            Karakorum Highway is located in the Karakoram range

            Karakorum highway in Pakistan starts from Hassan Abdal

            Karakorum highway is also called N-35

            Which university is near Imam Bari Shrine?

            Correct Answer: Quaid-i-Azam University
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            • The shrine was originally built on the orders of Mughal emperor Aurangzeb.

            Hingol river is situated in which province?

            Correct Answer: Baluchistan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            24 rivers are present in Pakistan

            • 5 rivers flow in Punjab Province
            • 4 rivers flow in Sindh Province
            • 7 rivers flow in Balochistan Province
            • 8 rivers flow in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Province

            Who was the Father of Slave dynasty?

            Correct Answer: Qutb-ud-din Aibak
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Qutab ud Din Aibak was the first ruler of the Muslim dynasty in Sub-Continent

            ......

            Qutb al-Din Aibak was a general of Muhammad Ghori.

            Qutab ud Din Aibak was in charge of the Ghurid territories in northern India, and after Muhammad Ghori's death.

            he became the ruler of an independent kingdom that evolved into the Delhi Sultanate ruled by the Mamluk dynasty.

            Qutab ud Din Aibak is buried in Lahore. 

            .....


            The Lodhi Dynasty was the last dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate.

            It was ruling from 1451 until the defeat by Babur in 1526, which led to the establishment of the Mughal Empire.

            The first dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate was the Mamluk Dynasty (also known as the Slave Dynasty), which began with Qutb al-Din Aibak in 1206.


            The first dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate was the Mamluk Dynasty (also known as the Slave Dynasty), which began with Qutb al-Din Aibak in 1206.

            ....

            Al-khafif was the first ruler of soomra dynasty (1011-1026)

            Mughal emperor Akbar the first Muslim ruler who tried to reconcile the differences between Muslims and Hindus

            Razia Sultana the first woman ruler of India

            221

            It was compulsory for Mansabdar to hold at least horsemen?

            Correct Answer: 200-400
            Show explanation

            It was indicated by their 'Sawar' rank, in the Mughal Empire

            Mansabdari system was introduced by Mughal king Akbar in 1571

            222

            Akbar's ruling period is as under:

            Correct Answer: 1556-1605
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Akbar, the third Mughal emperor, ruled from 1556 to 1605.

            He was crowned Emperor at the age of 13

            After the death of his father Humayun in 1556.

            He was born on October 15, 1542 in Umarko

            He died on October 25, 1605, Agra, India

            223

            Potwar plateau and salt range region lies__ between and ___rivers.

            Correct Answer: Indus & Jhelum
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            The Pothohar Plateau also known as Pothwar, is a plateau in north-eastern Pakistan

            The Pothohar plateau is rich in minerals like gypsum, limestone, coal, oil, and rock salt.

            Located between the Indus River and the Jhelum River
            ******

            • The Potwar Plateau includes parts of the current four districts:

              Jhelum

              Chakwal

              Rawalpindi

              Attock

              ****

              The Pothohar plateau is rich in minerals like gypsum, limestone, coal, oil, and rock salt.

              Potohar Plateau سطح مرتفع پوٹھوہار .consists of:

              • District Rawalpindi District
              • Chakwal District
              • Jhelum District
              • Khushab District,
              • Mirpur District
              • Islamabad
              • Mianwali District,
              • Sargodha District (Northern Belt)



            224

            When Mughal King Humayun recaptured Delhi Darbar?

            Correct Answer: 1555
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Humayun recaptured Delhi in 1555, with the help of the Persian king Shah Tahmasp, after being in exile for 15 years.

            In 1545, Humayun captured Kandhar and Kabul with Persian help.

            Humayun defeated his brothers Kamran and Askari.

            Humayun ruled from 1530 to 1540 and again from 1555 to 1556.

            225

            Cabinet Mission Plan was announced in the year ______?

            Correct Answer: February 1946
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Cabinet Mission was sent to India in February 1946.

            They arrived in India on March 24, 1946.

            The Cabinet Mission Plan was announced on May 16, 1946.

            The plan was created by the Cabinet Mission, which was sent to India by British Prime Minister Clement Attlee to discuss transferring power from the British to the Indians.

            The mission's members (3) were Lord Pethick-Lawrence, Sir Stafford Cripps, and A. V. Alexander. 

            226

            Indus Basin Treaty of 1960 between Pakistan and India is related to _______ problem.

            Correct Answer: Water
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • The Indus Waters Treaty, signed on September 19, 1960, between India and Pakistan and brokered by the World Bank.
              • It allocates the Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to Pakistan and the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India.
              • It was signed in Karachi by Pakistan President Ayub Khan, Indian Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru, and Mr. W.A.B. Illif. 
              • The Treaty became effective on April 1, 1960.
              227

              What is the length of the border between Pakistan and Afghanistan?

              Correct Answer: 2611 km
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The length of the common border between Pakistan and Afghanistan is 2611 km

              Old length between Pak vs Afghistan is 2430

              ...

              The common border between Pakistan and Afghanistan is called Durand Line

              ..

              The Durand line is drawn in 1893 between Mortimer Durand and Abdul Rahman

              Durand line agreement between Mortimer Durand and Abdul Rahman.

              ********

              According to the Survey of Pakistan 2021, the border between Pakistan and Afghanistan is 2611 km


              228

              Khyber is a _____?

              Correct Answer: Pass
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Important Passes of Pakistan

              1. Khyber Pass 

              connects: Peshawar to Kabul

              connects: Pakistan to Afghanistan

              Height 1070 meters, 3510 feet 


              2. Bolan Pass 

              Connects: JCB and Sibi with Quetta 

              Height: 1793 meters, 5884 feet 


              3. Lowari Pass 

              Connects: Chitral with Dir 

              Height: 3118 meters, 10230 feet 


              4. Broghil Pass 

              Location: Pakistan & Afghanistan border 

              Height: 3798 meters, 12460 feet 


              5. Malakand Pass 

              Connects: Dir with Chitral 

              Height: 1362 meters, 4468 feet 


              6. Shandur pass

              Connects: Gupis/Gilgit with Chitral 

              Height: 3700 meters, 12139 feet 


              7. Khanjerab Pass (topmost in Pakistan) 

              Other name: Friendship Gate

              Connects: China with Pakistan 

              Height: 4693 meters, 15397 feet 


              8. Dorah pass

              Connects: Badakhshan province of Afghanistan with Chitral 

              Height: 4300 meters, 14108 feet 


              9. Babusar Pass 

              Connects: Mansehra/Abbottabad with Gilgit


              10. Kohat Pass

              Connects: Kohat with Peshawar 


              11. Khojak Pass

              Connects: Qilla Abdullah with Chaman


              12. Tochi Pass

              Connects: Ghazni with Bannu


              13. Karakar Pass

              Connects: Mingora with Buner 


              229

              In which city National School of Public Policy located?

              Correct Answer: Lahore
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The National Management College is located at Lahore, Pakistan.

              It is a constituent unit of National School of Public Policy (NSPP).

              The main NSPP campus is in Lahore.

              NSPP is led by a Rector.

              He typically a senior officer from the Pakistan Administrative Service who has served as a Federal Secretary.

              The Rector's position, governed by the NSPP Ordinance 2002, is a four-year term.

              230

              How many districts in Ladakh?

              Correct Answer: 2
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Union Territory of Ladakh has two districts: Leh and Kargil.

              However, the Government of India announced plans to increase the number of districts to seven by 2028.

              The new districts will be Zanskar, Drass, Sham, Nubra, and Changthang.

              Leh is the largest district in India by area.

              It's also one of the world's coldest and most elevated inhabited regions. 

              231

              Rameez Raja has served as the _______ Chairman of the Pakistan Cricket Board?

              Correct Answer: 35th
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Ramiz Raja has served as the 35th chairman of the Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB).


              In office: 13 September 2021 – 21 December 2022

              Appointed by: Imran Khan

              President: Arif Alvi

              Preceded by: Ehsan Mani

              Succeeded by: Najam Sethi

              232

              Who visited Saudi Arabia to resist in conditions between countries brief to PM Imran Khan ago and after agreeing to visit Saudi Arabia?

              Correct Answer: Qamar Javed Bajwa
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan’s Prime Minister Imran Khan has started a three-day visit (May 7-9, 2021) to Saudi Arabia in an effort to reset relations after tensions between the longtime allies in recent years.

              233

              The Ojhri Camp incident occurred in which year?

              Correct Answer: 1988
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Ojhri Camp was a military storage center located in Rawalpindi Military District in Rawalpindi.

              The Ojhri Camp disaster took place on April 10, 1988, when an ammunition depot in Rawalpindi exploded.

              It causing widespread destruction.

              234

              Keti Bandar Port is located in ______?

              Correct Answer: Sindh
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Keti Bandar Port is located in Sindh

              Katti Bandar port is located in the Thatta district

              Keti Bandar small town in Sindh is famous for Fish Harbour

              The Keti Bandar Port  is a port on the Arabian Sea

              The port was built on the remains of the older seaport of Debal where Muhammad bin Qasim and his army arrived from Iraq.
              ND16-3-2023

              235

              Which player remained champion in squash for 5 years?

              Correct Answer: Jahangir Khan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistani squash player Jahangir Khan became a legend in his sport by going undefeated for five years.

              He won world open title six times.

              He also won 555 matches.

              236

              Hassan Habib is associated with which sport?

              Correct Answer: Poker
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Hasan Habib is a Pakistani American professional poker player.

              He was born on 19 April 1962 in Karachi.

              237

              Who was the first ruler of slave Dynasty?

              Correct Answer: Qutab ud Din Aibak
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Qutab ud Din Aibak was the first ruler of the Muslim dynasty in Sub-Continent

              ......

              Qutb al-Din Aibak  was a general of Muhammad Ghori.

              Qutab ud Din Aibak was in charge of the Ghurid territories in northern India, and after Muhammad Ghori's death.

              he became the ruler of an independent kingdom that evolved into the Delhi Sultanate ruled by the Mamluk dynasty.

              Qutab ud Din Aibak is buried in Lahore. 

              .....


              The Lodhi Dynasty was the last dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate.

              It was ruling from 1451 until the defeat by Babur in 1526, which led to the establishment of the Mughal Empire.

              The first dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate was the Mamluk Dynasty (also known as the Slave Dynasty), which began with Qutb al-Din Aibak in 1206.


              The first dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate was the Mamluk Dynasty (also known as the Slave Dynasty), which began with Qutb al-Din Aibak in 1206.

              ....

              Al-khafif was the first ruler of soomra dynasty (1011-1026)

              Mughal emperor Akbar the first Muslim ruler who tried to reconcile the differences between Muslims and Hindus

              Razia Sultana the first woman ruler of Indiar

              238

              “Pakistan: The Formative Phase, 1857-1948” is written by ________?

              Correct Answer: Khalid Bin Sayeed
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan: The Formative Phase, 1857-1948” published in 1968.

              239

              Which famous actor debuted from the drama "Umrao Jaan Ada" in 2003?

              Correct Answer: Imran Abbas
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Umrao Jaan Ada is an Urdu novel by Mirza Hadi Ruswa (1857–1931).

              It was first published in 1899.

              It is considered the first Urdu novel by many scholars.

              The story revolves around a tawaif and poet named Umrao Jaan Ada from 19th-century Lucknow.

              240

              What did the AJK (Azad Jammu and Kashmir) frontier people want during the 1947 independence?

              Correct Answer: Independent from Pakistan and India
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              During the 1947 independence, some people in the Azad Jammu and Kashmir (AJK) frontier region desired independence from both Pakistan and India.

              The All Jammu and Kashmir Muslim Conference passed a resolution in Srinagar on July 19, 1947 to join Pakistan.

              241

              Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan got fame due to his association with ______?

              Correct Answer: Qawali
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan born October 1948.

              King of Qawwali, Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan died on August 16, 1997 of a sudden cardiac arrest in London at the age of 48.

              242

              Mound of Dead is also known as _____?

              Correct Answer: Mohenjo-Daro
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Mohenjo-Daro and Harappa is located in present-day Sindh and Punjab, Pakistan,


              Additional information 

              Mohenjo-Daro means Mound of the Dead

              This is because after it had declined, the remains started to have layers over them.

              it was first recognized in 1922

              It was discovered by R. D. Banerji

              It is located in Larkana upper Sindh

              It was the largest settlement of the ancient Indus Valley Civilisation

              243

              Hingol Dam is situated in?

              Correct Answer: Baluchistan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Hingol Dam is a proposed 3.5 MW hydroelectric dam on the Hingol River in Lasbela District, Balochistan.

              It is near Hingol National Park.

              It is a scenic tourist spot known for its wildlife, including the rare Houbara bustard.

              244

              Who was the incumbent SAPM on CPEC in 2021?

              Correct Answer: Khalid Mansoor
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Khalid Mansoor was the Special Assistant to the Prime Minister of Pakistan on the China-Pakistan Economic Corridor, a position he has held from August 2021 to April 2022.

              He previously served as the Chief Executive of the Hub Power Company.

              245

              Rawalpindi Conspiracy Case was unearthed in which year?

              Correct Answer: 1951
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Rawalpindi Conspiracy (also known as the Rawalpindi Conspiracy Case)

              It was unearthed in 1951.

              It was an attempted coup d'état against the government of Liaquat Ali Khan

              At  this time Liaquat Ali Khan, the first Prime Minister of Pakistan, in 1951

              The hearing of the Rawalpindi Conspiracy Case began on the 14th of June 1951 in Hyderabad Jail

              Faiz Ahmad Faiz and Major General Akbar Khan were part of the Rawalpindi Conspiracy Case

              ND1-5-2023

              246

              What is the name of first woman Deputy chairperson of Senate of Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Dr Noor Jahan Panezai
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              About Senate of Pakistan


              Senate: Upper House of Pakistan

              Senate in Urdu: ایوان بالا

              Formation: 1973

              Original members: 45

              Raised to 63: 1977

              Raised 87: 2 March, 1985

              Raised to 100: 2002

              First Hindu Woman Senator: Kirshna Kumari

              Half of the Senate members retire after: 3 years

              Members term: 6 years

              Head of Senate is called: Chairman

              National Assembly & Senate: Makes up the Parliament of Pakistan

              Seats for each province: 23

              Baluchistan Women Seats: 4

              Sindh Women Seats: 4

              KPK Women Seats: 4

              Punjab Women Seats: 4

              ICT Women Seat: 1

              Total Women Seats: 17

              Senate elections: after 3 years

              First woman deputy chairperson: Dr Noor Jahan Panezai

              Youngest chairman senate: Sadiq Sanjrani

              First chairman Senate: Habib Ullah Khan




              247

              Which Indian pilot was captured in 2019 by Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Abhinandan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan Air Force celebrates Operation Swift Retort as Surprise on 27 February since 2020.

              After 14 February 2019, when suicide bomber attacked CRPF convoy in Pulwama, killing 40 CRPF soldiers, tensions between India and Pakistan escalated.

              Later on February 26, 2019 Indian Air Force (IAF) carried out counter-terror operation (Balakot Air Strike), hitting in Balakot.

              It was on February 27, 2019, when PAF exhibited supreme professionalism by shooting down two Indian Air Force jets, a MiG-21 Bison and a SU-30. The downed MiG-21 pilot, wing commander Abhinandan, whose aircraft had crashed inside Pakistan’s territory was captured.

              Abhinandan was later released and handed over to India on March 1, 2019.

              248

              Pakistan ideology based on which theory?

              Correct Answer: Two nation theory
              Show explanation
              Explanation
            • Pakistan's ideology is based on the Two Nation Theory, which asserts that Muslims and Hindus are distinct nations with their own religions, cultures, and values.
            • This theory was the foundation for the demand for a separate Muslim state, leading to the creation of Pakistan in 1947.
              • 249

              Who was appointed first secretary of the Board of Trustees of Aligarh College?

              Correct Answer: Sir Syed Ahmad Khan
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Sir Syed Ahmed Khan Got Knowledge From Farid Ud Din (Maternal-grandfather – Ex Mughal Pm)
              • Joined Gov’t In 1839  => Clerical Job naib munshi (administrative assistant)
              • Promoted As Sub-judge 1841
              • Transferred To Delhi  Chief Judge In 1846 
              • Member Of Imperial Council 1886



              • Aligarh Movement Objective:
              • 1. Modern Education For Muslims To Compete Hindus 
              • 2. Cooperation With The British Government


              • Established:

              • 1. Schools   • Muradabad (1859)  • Ghazipur (1863)
              • 2. Scientific Society At Gahazipur (1864)
              • 3. Muhammadan Educational Conference 1866
              • 4. Muhammadan Anglo-oriental College =>
              • Mao High School was Established in 1875
              • Got Status Of College 1877  
              • Status Of University 1920
              250

              The Faraizi Movement started by Haji Shariatullah on his return from Saudi Arabia, was first initiated in:

              Correct Answer: East Bengal
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Faraizi movement started in East Bengal in 1818 led by Haji Shariatullah.

              251

              Bradlaugh Hall has been the venue of some of the most important events in the history of Pakistan and India. In which city is it located?

              Correct Answer: Lahore
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Bradlaugh Hall is a historic hall located in Lahore, Punjab, Pakistan.

              It was founded in the memory of a British member of the parliament, Charles Bradlaugh.

              It hosted the meetings of the Congress during the Indian independence movement.

              252

              Which of the following are not members of the Council of Common Interests according to the 1973 constitution?

              Correct Answer: Prime Minister
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              According to the 1973 Constitution of Pakistan, the Council of Common Interests (CCI) comprises:

              • The Prime Minister of Pakistan (as chairperson)
              • The Chief Ministers of the provinces (Punjab, Sindh, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, and Balochistan)
              • The Governors of the provinces (as members)

              However, the Prime Minister is not a member of the CCI.

              253

              Ibn Battuta visited India during the reign of______________?

              Correct Answer: Muhammad bin Tughlaq
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Ibn Battuta was: a traveler

              Ibn Battuta belonged: Morocco

              254

              Sachal Sarmast was a famous poet of which language?

              Correct Answer: Sindhi
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Hazrat Sachal Sarmast born 1152 Hijir (1739 CE)

              Hazrat Sachal Sarmast died 1242 Hijri (1827 CE)

              Hazrat Sachal Sarmast shrine in Ranipur (Sindh)

              He was an 18th and 19th-century Sufi poet

              He is a poet of the Sindhi-language literature.

              He is known: as a poet of seven languages (شاعر ہفت زبان)

              255

              According to the 1973 constitution, what is the term of the office of President?

              Correct Answer: 5 years
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              According to the constitution's 1973, minimum age for President of Pakistan: 45 Years

              The president is elected indirectly for a term of five years.

              The incumbent president is eligible for re-election to that office, but cannot hold that office for more than two consecutive terms.

              ND22-6-2023
              256

              In the early 1970s, Pakistan played a role in rapprochement between which two countries?

              Correct Answer: US and China
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan acted as a mediator to facilitate secret diplomatic communications between the US and China.

              This led to Henry Kissinger's secret visit to China in 1971.

              257

              Thal Nova is a project of which sector?

              Correct Answer: Energy
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              ThalNova will be the second coal-fired power plant to be set up in Tharparker district of Sindh

              This was followed by the signing of a shareholder agreement in 2018 between:

              • Thal Limited (House of Habib)
              • Novatex (a subsidiary of Gani & Tayub)
              • Hub Power Holdings Limited (HUBCO) 
              258

              Emperor Humayun was ousted by Sher Shah Suri and lived in exile for how many years?

              Correct Answer: 15 Years
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Humayun (1530 AD-1540 AD and 1555 AD-1556 AD):

              • He was the son of Babur and ascended the throne in 1530 AD.
              • His succession was challenged by his brothers Kamran, Hindal and Askari along with the Afghans.
              • His first campaign was against Kalinjar.
              • The Battle of Chausa (1539 AD) was fought between Sher Shah and Humayun.
              259

              What is the climate fund required by 2030 as per the NCFS (National Climate Finance Strategy)?

              Correct Answer: $348 billion
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The National Climate Finance Strategy (NCFS) of Pakistan estimates

              The country will need $348 billion in climate finance by 2030 to achieve its climate-resilient development goals


              Pakistan faces a significant climate finance gap due to its vulnerability to climate change.

              The 2022 floods alone caused an estimated $30 billion in damage.

              260

              Which Amendment made in the constitution, on April 1974, defines the boundaries of Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: First
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The First Amendment to the Constitution of Pakistan, enacted on May 4, 1974, redefined Pakistan's boundaries and eliminated references to East Pakistan.

              The amendment also redefined Pakistan's federal and naval treaties.

              The First Amendment was introduced during the tenure of Zulfikar Ali Bhutto to protect against internal and external destabilization.

              261

              The Pakistan Badminton Federation formed in ______?

              Correct Answer: 1953
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Pakistan Badminton Federation (PBF) was formed in 1953.

              The PBF is the national governing body for badminton in Pakistan.

              It was responsible for the management and development of the sport.

              The Pakistan national badminton team was also established in 1953.

              262

              Who was the Leiutenant-Governor of the North-Western Provinces in 1895-1896?

              Correct Answer: Sir Anthony Patrick
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Sir Anthony Patrick MacDonnell was the Lieutenant-Governor of the North-Western Provinces in 1895-1896.

              He was a prominent British administrator in India known for his reforms in the provinces.

              He particularly focusing on famine relief and public administration.

              263

              Pakistani actor Fawad Khan acted in which Bollywood film?

              Correct Answer: Khoobsurat
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Fawad Khan made his Bollywood debut in the film Khoobsurat (2014) alongside Sonam Kapoor.

              Khan has also acted in the following films: Kapoor & Sons (2016) and Armaan (2013).

              264

              Daily Dawn was published by Quaid-e-Azam in _____?

              Correct Answer: 1942
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Dawn was founded by Quaid-i-Azam Mohammad Ali Jinnah in Delhi, India, on October 26, 1941, as a mouthpiece for the Muslim League.

              Dawn begun as a weekly newspaper in 1941 and transformed into a daily in 1942.

              265

              Who was the first Pakistani boxer to win WBC Silver Flyweight Championship in 2016?

              Correct Answer: Muhammad Waseem
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Muhammad Waseem was the first Pakistani boxer to win the WBC Silver Flyweight Championship in 2016.

              He defeated Jether Oliva by unanimous decision on July 17, 2016.

              Waseem successfully defended his title on November 27, 2016 against Giemel Magramo.

              266

              The Electoral College of Pakistan for the election of President of Pakistan consists of _____?

              Correct Answer: The Members of both Houses and the Members of the provincial assemblies
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The President of Pakistan is chosen by an electoral college, in Pakistan.

              According to Article 41(3) of the Constitution of Pakistan, this electoral college consists of:

              • the Senate
              • the National Assembly of Pakistan
              • the Provincial Assemblies of the four provinces.


              There are currently 538 electors in the Electoral College.

              A majority of 270 electoral votes is required to elect the president.

              267

              Which team was the winner of Pakistan Super League (PSL) 2018?

              Correct Answer: Islamabad United
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan Super League (PSL) 2018

              Edition: Third

              Dates: 22 Feb–25 Mar 2018

              Administrator: PCB

              Format: Twenty20

              Venue: UAE & Pakistan

              Winner: Islamabad United (2nd title)

              Runners-up: Peshawar Zalmi

              Islamabad United won by: 3 wickets

              Participants: 6

              Matches: 34

              Player of the series: Luke Ronchi (Islamabad United)

              Luke Ronchi made: 435 runs

              Most runs: Luke Ronchi

              Best Bowler Award: Faheem Ashraf (Islamabad United)

               

               

              268

              Malik Ghulam Muhammad abolished the Assembly of _____?

              Correct Answer: Muhammad Ali Bogra
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Malik Ghulam Muhammad, the Governor-General of Pakistan, dissolved the assembly under Muhammad Ali Bogra's government in 1954 due to political conflicts.

              The Constituent Assembly of Pakistan was dissolved on October 24, 1954, by Governor General Malik Ghulam Muhammad.

              269

              Which Pakistani Prime Minister also served as President of Pakistan Hockey Federation?

              Correct Answer: Mir Zafarullah Khan Jamali
              Show explanation
              Explanation
            • Mir Zafarullah Khan Jamali was also an ex-hockey player and the former President of the Pakistan Hockey Federation.
            • Jamali, a veteran Pakistani politician, passed away at the age of 76.
            • He served as the 15th Prime Minister of Pakistan from 2002 to 2004.
              • 270

              Where is Astore district located?

              Correct Answer: Gilgit-Baltistan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Astore District is a district of Pakistan-administered Gilgit-Baltistan in the disputed Kashmir region.

              It is one of the 14 districts of the Pakistani-administered territory of Gilgit−Baltistan.

              Its administrative headquarters are located at Eidgah in the Astore Valley.

              271

              How many times did Pakistan become Olympic Champion in Hockey?

              Correct Answer: Three
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan's men's national field hockey team has won three Olympic gold medals in field hockey: 

              • Rome 1960
              • Mexico City 1968
              • Los Angeles 1984

              They have also won three silver medals (1956, 1964, and 1972), and two bronze medals (1976 and 1992).

              272

              The Unionist Party (Punjab) was formed in ________?

              Correct Answer: 1923
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The National Unionist Party was a political party based in the Punjab Province

              This party was enforced during the period of British rule in India.

              In the 1937 Indian provincial elections, the Unionist Party soundly defeated the Muslim League in Punjab.

              Unionist Party won 98 seats (out of 175 total), including 78 of the 89 Muslim seats,

              while the Muslim League won only two

              members of the party

              • Sir Sikandar Hayat Khan
              • Sir Khizar Hayat Tiwana
              • Sir Fazli Husain
              • Sir Shahab-ud-Din
              •  Muhammad Hussain Shah
              • Sir Chhotu Ram
              273

              What is the second-largest city of Punjab by population?

              Correct Answer: Faisalabad
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Karachi is the most populous city in Pakistan

              Lahore is the second most populous city in Pakistan and the most populous city of Punjab.

              Punjab is the most populous province of Pakistan,

              containing more than half the country’s total population as well as several of its major cities: Lahore, Faisalabad, Rawalpindi, Multān, and Gujranwala.

              **

              IMPORTANT QUESTION RELATED TO "PUNJAB"

              The area of Punjab is____ Square kilometers.

              205,345

              The Area of Punjab is____ percent of the total area of Pakistan.
              26%  (25.8%)

              The population of Punjab is____ percent of the total population of Pakistan.
              56% (about 110,012,442 as of 2017)

              Punjab is the ____area-wise?
              Second largest

              There are ____districts in Punjab.
              36

              There are ____Divisions in Punjab.
              09

              Punjab occupies the ____part of Pakistan.
              Northeastern

              Which is the most populous city of Punjab?

              Lahore

              275

              Chauburji in Lahore was built by _____?

              Correct Answer: Shah Jahan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Chauburji is a Mughal era monument in the city of Lahore, capital of the Pakistani province of Punjab.

              The monument was built in 1646 C.E. during the reign of the emperor Shah Jahan.

              It previously acted as a gateway to a large garden.

              276

              University of Karachi was established in ______?

              Correct Answer: 1951
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The University of Karachi was established on June 23, 1951.

              It is one of the oldest and largest universities in Pakistan.

              It offering a wide range of academic programs.

              277

              In 1942 Gandhi launched _____?

              Correct Answer: Quit India Movement
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Know everything about Quit India Movement

              Started: Aug 8, 1942

              Started from: Bombay 

              Launched by: Gandhi

              Also known as: August Movement 

              Demand: to end British rule

              Jinnah changed the slogan "Divide and Quit India" 

              Started by party: Congress
