              278

              Shalimar Garden, Lahore is an important feature of ____ garden.

              Correct Answer: Mughal
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan built Shalimar Gardens in Lahore.

              Its construction was started in the year 1647 and completed in 1959

              It is included in World Heritage Site: 1981

              Its also known as the Jamia Masjid of Thatta



              Sr#

              Built by

              Name of Places

              1

              Jahangir​

              Shalimar Bagh, Kashmir

              2

              Shah Jahan

              1. Shalimar Garden, Pakistan,

               

              2. Taj Mahal,

              3. the Jama Masjid,

              4. the Red Fort

              3

              Akbar

              1.    Buland Darwaza,

              2.    Fatehpur Sikri

              4

              Aurangzeb

              Moti Masjid, Delhi


              279

              On how many balls Shahid Afridi hit the fastest century?

              Correct Answer: 37
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistani legendary cricketer Shahid Afridi hit a century against Sri Lanka on 37 balls in the year 1996.



              AGM 24-12-2022



              280

              Satpara lake is located in which region of Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Skardu
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Satpara Lake is a natural lake near Skardu, Gilgit-Baltistan.

              It is fed by the Satpara Stream.

              It is situated at an elevation of 2,636 meters above sea level

              It is spread over an area of 2.5 km².

              281

              ولی دکنی کو ولی دکنی کو جمال دوست کا لقب کسی محقق نے دیا؟

              Correct Answer: ڈاکٹر سید عبد الله
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              ولی دکنی ایک مشہور اُردو شاعر تھے جو دکن کے علاقے سے تعلق رکھتے تھے اور اُردو شاعری کے ابتدائی نمائندوں میں شامل ہیں۔
              ان کی شاعری میں سادگی اور روانی تھی، اور وہ اُردو کے پہلے معیاری شاعر سمجھے جاتے ہیں۔
              ڈاکٹر سید عبد اللہ نے ولی دکنی کی شاعری کی تحقیق کرتے ہوئے انہیں "جمال دوست" کا لقب دیا، جو ان کے ادب کی جمالیاتی اور دوستانہ خصوصیات کو ظاہر کرتا ہے

              282

              Ghulam Muhammad Barrage was constructed on river _____?

              Correct Answer: Indus
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Ghulam Muhammad Barrage is also known as Kotri Barrage.

              It was inaugurated by the third governor general of Pakistan General Ghulam Muhammad Malik.

               The barrage was completed in 1955.

              It is used to control water flow in the Indus for irrigation and flood control purposes.

              Opened: 1955

              Length: 1,600m 

              283

              Which among the following is the oldest city of Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Multan
              Show explanation
              Explanation
            • Multan is considered the oldest city in Pakistan, with a history dating back over 5,000 years.
            • It is known as the "City of Saints" due to its historical and cultural significance in the region.
              • 284

              Partition of Bengal was annulled after _____?

              Correct Answer: 6 years
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Lord Curzon divided Bengal into two provinces on 16-10-1905

              on October 16, 1905, by Lord Curzon, the British colonial administrator of India at the time

              The partition of Bengal was the most important event during the rule of Lord Curzon

              The province was divided into West Bengal, which had a majority of Hindus,

              and East Bengal, which had a majority of Muslims

              ****

              285

              Which Pakistani Cricket Player became the 8th international player to score 194 runs?

              Correct Answer: Saeed Anwar
              Show explanation
              Explanation
            • Saeed Anwar scored 194 runs against India in Chennai on May 21, 1997.
            • This innings made him the first Pakistani and 8th international player to achieve such a high score in ODIs at the time.
              • 286

              Asghar Khan became the Air Marshal of Pakistan in _____?

              Correct Answer: 1957
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • The first Muslim Commander-in-Chief of Pakistan Air Force was Air Marshal Asghar Khan.
              • He held this position from 1957 to 1965.
              287

              Which among the following were the characters of TV Drama Waris?

              Correct Answer: All of these
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Mehboob Alam as Dilawar has performed in one of the lead roles of Waris

              Directed by: Nusrat Thakur; Ghazanfer Ali

              Written by: Amjad Islam Amjad

              No. of episodes 13

              Distributor PTV

              language: Urdu

              country of origin: Pakistan

              The first episode was aired from PTV-Lahore on Saturday 29 December 1979 and the last on 22 March 1980 

              288

              Who among the following got Pride of Performance on Naat reciting?

              Correct Answer: M Azam Chisti
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              In 1979, Muhammad Azam Chishti received the Pride of Performance award for Naat recitation.
              The award was presented by former               President of Pakistan, Zia-ul-Haq.

              289

              Ustaad Bade Fateh Ali Khan was awarded Pride of Performance in _____?

              Correct Answer: 1969
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Ustad Bade Fateh Ali Khan was born on 20 April 1935 in Hoshiarpur, Punjab Province of British India.

              He was honored with the Pride of Performance Award by the President of Pakistan in 1969.

              He was passed away on 4 January 2017 at the age of 82.

              290

              When did Pakistan sign convention on the Rights of Persons with disabilities?

              Correct Answer: 2008
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan signed the Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities (CRPD) on 25th September 2008.

              The CRPD ensures the protection and promotion of the rights of persons with disabilities, focusing on equality, accessibility, and inclusion.

              291

              Who is the only Indian who received the highest civilian awards from both Pakistan and India; Nishan-e-Pakistan and Bharat Ratna?

              Correct Answer: Morarji Desai
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Morarji Desai is the only Indian who received the highest civilian awards from both India and Pakistan, the Bharat Ratna and Nishaan-e-Pakistan.

              He was the 4th PM (1977-1979) of India.

              He was the second deputy prime minister of India.

              He was the first non-congress Indian Prime Minister.

              292

              Which Pakistani Sipahi came back from India after remaining in Indian prison for 40 years and died in 2018?

              Correct Answer: Maqbool Hussain
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Maqbool Hussain, a Pakistani soldier injured and captured by the Indian Army during the 1965 war.

              He sent 40 years in Indian jails before being released as a civilian prisoner in 2005.

              He was born in 1940.

              He was died on August 28, 2018.

              293

              Third battle of Panipat was fought between ______?

              Correct Answer: Marahatas and Afghans
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Fought on January 14, 1761

              Location: Panipat, India

              Result: Durrani victory

              1st battle of Panipat: 1526

              2nd battle of Panipat: 1556

              294

              Wazir Mansion is the birth place of which person?

              Correct Answer: Quaid-e-Azam
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Wazir Mansion is the birth place of Quaid-i-Azam.

              It was built during 1860 to 1870 with stone masonry, lime and jute.

              It is located in the area of Kharadar, Karachi.

              295

              Who among the following is known as Falcon of Andalus?

              Correct Answer: Abdul Rahman I
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Abd al-Rahman I was the founder and first emir of the Emirate of Córdoba, ruling from 756 to 788.

              He established the Umayyad dynasty in al-Andalus.

              296

              When did Musharraf take the oath of President of Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Before the Agra Summit
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Musharraf took the oath of President on June 20, 2001, just a month before the Agra Summit in July 2001.

              297

              In 1913 which mosque was demolished and many Muslim were killed?

              Correct Answer: Kanpur Mosque
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Kanpur mosque situated in Kanpur, India 

              Rioters demolished it during Hindu-Muslim riots.

              The Kanpur mosque tragedy took place in 1913.

              298

              At the time of Independence which community was in majority in the state of Kashmir?

              Correct Answer: Muslim
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • At the time of Independence Muslim, the community was in majority in the state of Kashmir
              • ****
              • British government sold Kashmir for a sum of 7.5 million Nanakshahee rupees to Gulab Singh, hereafter bestowed with the title of Maharaja.
              • *****
              • In the last census of British India in 1941, the total population of Kashmir and Jammu was 3,945,000.
              • 1.The total Muslim population was 2,997,000 (75.97%),
              • 2 The Hindu population was 808,000 (20.48%),
              • 3.The Sikh 55,000 (1.39%)
              299

              What is the other name of Mohammad Ali Bogra Formula?

              Correct Answer: Constitutional Formula
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Constitutional Formulathe another name of Mohammad Ali Bogra Formula

              The Bogra Formula was a political compromise presented and proposed by Prime Minister Bogra on 7 October 1953

              The Bogra Formula sought to permanently abolish the Governor-General and to be replaced with the elected figurehead presidency.

              Mohammad Ali Bogra was the 2nd Bengali to serve as Prime Minister of Pakistan.

              Sahibzada Mohammad Ali Bogra was the 3rd Prime Minister of Pakistan from 17 April 1953 till 12 August 1955.

              He was the 3rd & 7th Minister of Foreign Affairs from 13 June 1962 till 23 January 1963

              Bogra was Minister of Defence from 17 April 1953 to 24 October 1954.

              ND5-07-2023

              ND17-7-2023
              300

              Who was Mohammad Ali Bogra?

              Correct Answer: Prime Minister
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Mohammad Ali Bogra was the 2nd Bengali to serve as Prime Minister of Pakistan.

              Sahibzada Mohammad Ali Bogra was the 3rd Prime Minister of Pakistan from 17 April 1953 till 12 August 1955.

              He was the 3rd & 7th Minister of Foreign Affairs from 13 June 1962 till 23 January 1963

              Bogra was Minister of Defence from 17 April 1953 to 24 October 1954.

              ******

              ND30-12-2022

              ****

              ND10-1-2023

              SH26-8-2023

              301

              Article 58(2b) of constitution 1973 is about _____?

              Correct Answer: Power of President to dissolve National Assembly
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              These powers included the right, expressed in sub-section 2(b) inserted into Article 58, to dissolve the National Assembly (but not the Senate)

              if, in his or her opinion, "a situation has arisen in which the Government of the Federation cannot be carried on in accordance with the provisions of the Constitution
              302

              How many corps of Pakistan Army?

              Correct Answer: 9
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The structure of the Pakistan Army is based on two distinct themes: operational and administrative.

              Operationally the Pakistan Army is divided into nine corps.

              303

              The fourth Anglo-Mysore was started in ______?

              Correct Answer: 1798
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Fourth Anglo-Mysore War began in 1798 between the British East India Company and the Kingdom of Mysore, led by Tipu Sultan.

              It concluded in 1799 with the defeat of Mysore and the death of Tipu Sultan.

              304

              Somnath temple was destroyed in _____?

              Correct Answer: 1026
              Show explanation
              Explanation
            • In 1026, during the reign of Bhima I, Mahmud of Ghazni raided the Somnath temple in Gujarat, India.
            • He plundered the temple, broke its jyotirlinga, and looted a massive treasure worth 20 million dinars.
              • 305

              What is the old name of Lahore?

              Correct Answer: Mahmood pur
              Show explanation
              Explanation


              306

              Kalabagh is famous for the mineral of ______?

              Correct Answer: Iron
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Kalabagh is a town and union council of Mianwali District in the Punjab province of Pakistan. 
                • It is renowned for its rich deposits of iron ore, a significant resource for the steel industry.
                • The iron ore from Kalabagh is of high quality.
                307

                Malik Ishaq was the founder of ______?

                Correct Answer: Lashkar-e-Jhangvi
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Malik Ishaq co-founded Lashkar-e-Jhangvi (LeJ) in 1996 as a splinter group of Sipah-e-Sahaba Pakistan (SSP).

                Malik Ishaq was killed in 2015 during a police encounter in Punjab, Pakistan.

                308

                Who was the first to break the Constitution, abolish both assemblies, and impose martial law in Pakistan?

                Correct Answer: Sikander Mirza
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                President Iskander Mirza was the first to break the Constitution, abolish both assemblies and impose martial law in Pakistan in 1958: 
                Date: On October 7, 1958, Mirza staged a coup detat 
                Actions: He: 
                • Abrogated the Constitution of Pakistan of 1956 
                • Imposed martial law 
                • Dissolved the National and Provincial Assemblies 
                • Abolished political parties 
                Aftermath: Three weeks later, on October 27, 1958, General Ayub Khan deposed Mirza and assumed the presidency.



                309

                What is the primary role of Parliament?

                Correct Answer: To make laws
                Show explanation
                Explanation
              • The primary role of Parliament is to make laws for the governance of a country.
              • Interpretation of laws (B) is the role of the judiciary.
              • Enforcement of laws (D) is the role of the executive branch.
                • 310

                What is the famous fort of Bahawalpur?

                Correct Answer: Derawar Fort
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Derawar Fort is located in the Cholistan Desert, Ahmadpur East Tehsil, Bahawalpur.

                The height of the walls of Derawar Fort is 40 meters

                The fort was built in the 9th century by Rai Jajja Bhatti, a Rajput ruler.

                The Nawabs of Bahawalpur took control of the fort in 1733

                311

                The Greater Thal Canal (GTC) draws water from the _____?

                Correct Answer: Chashma-Jhelum
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The Greater Thal Canal (GTC) draws water from the Chashma-Jhelum Link Canal, which diverts water from the Indus River.

                The GTC is a non-perennial irrigation scheme that provides water from April to September. The GTC's components include:

                • Main Canal: 35 km long
                • Mankera Branch Canal: 65 km long
                • Choubara Branch Canal: 73 km long
                • Dhingana Branch Canal: Includes the Mahmood Sub-Branch Canal and Noorpur Branch Canal systems 

                The GTC was inaugurated in 2001 by Pakistani President Pervez Musharraf. The second phase of the project, the Choubara Branch, was launched in 2020. 

                312

                القانون فی الطب کے مصنف ہیں؟

                Correct Answer: ابن سینا
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                 "القانون فی الطب" ابن سینا کی کتاب ہے۔

                یہ کتاب 1025 عیسوی میں لکھی گئی تھی۔

                "القانون فی الطب" طب کے میدان میں ایک مستند اور اہم کتاب ہے جسے قرون وسطیٰ کے دوران اسلامی دنیا

                اور یورپ میں طب کی تعلیم اور مشق میں بڑے پیمانے پر استعمال کیا گیا۔







                313

                Karakorum ranges is the ____ heightest mountain range in the world

                Correct Answer: 2
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The Karakoram Range is the second-highest mountain range in the world.

                The range's highest peak, K2, is the second-highest peak on Earth. 

                Highest peak: K2 (8,611 m or 28,251 ft)

                Their range spans the borders of China, India, and Pakistan.

                Karakoram has the highest peaks in Pakistan. 

                Karakoram is located in the North of the Himalayas range. 

                K2 is the highest peak of Karakoram

                K2 is mostly covered with ice. 

                Karakoram has less forest. 

                314

                On US and British move against Pakistan's so-called failure to block financing terrorists, the Financial Action Task Force in its meeting held in February this year has decided ______?

                Correct Answer: To reconsider the matter later
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The Financial Action Task Force (FATF) decided in February of this year to reconsider the matter later regarding the issue of Pakistan's alleged failure to curb financing for terrorism.

                This decision indicates that FATF will revisit the issue at a later date to assess Pakistan's compliance with international standards for combating money laundering and terrorist financing.

                315

                Who was the last ruler of Lodi dynasty?

                Correct Answer: Ibrahim Lodhi
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Ibrahim Lodhi (1517-1526) was the last ruler of the Lodi dynasty.

                He was the son of Sikandar Lodhi and succeeded him as the Sultan of Delhi.

                The list of rulers of the Lodi dynasty:

                1. Bahlol Lodhi (1451-1489)
                2. Sikandar Lodhi (1489-1517)
                3. Ibrahim Lodhi (1517-1526)


                Bahlul Khan Lodhi was the founder of the Lodhi dynasty of India (1451-1526)

                Bahlul Khan Lodhi was the founder of the Lodhi dynasty when he replaced the Sayyid dynasty.

                By the end of the 15th century, Bahlul Lodi firmly established the Lodi dynasty

                 The Lodhi dynasty It was the fifth and final dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate

                316

                Aligarh (MAO) Muslim College started by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan in 1877 had a clear programme _____?

                Correct Answer: To keep the Muslim community abreast with western knowledge
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                 

                • The Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College was founded by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan in 1875, initially as a primary school, with the intention of turning it into a college-level institution.
                • In 1877, the school was raised to college level.
                • Lord Lytton, the Viceroy of India at the time, laid the foundation stone of the Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College (MAO College) on January 8, 1878. The college was later upgraded to Aligarh Muslim University (AMU) in 1920.
                317

                Who became the first president of Anjumaan-I- Himayat-i-Islam?

                Correct Answer: Qazi Khalifa Hameed ud din
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Qazi Hameeduddin was originally from Tijara and born on 16 Sha'aban 1290 AH / 1873 AD. 

                ****

                Anjuman e Himayat e Islam was founded in September 1884.

                ****

                Who floated the idea of the establishment of Anjuman-e-Hamiat-e-Islam? 

                Munshi Charag ud Din.

                Who became the first president of Anjumaan-I- Himayat-i-Islam?

                Qazi Khalifa Hameed ud din 

                The Anjuman-i-Himayat-i-Islam was founded with the objective of?

                To defend Islam against the onslaughts of the  missionaries and provide western instructions education along with religious instructions


                ***

                RK151222



                318

                The mausoleum of Khawaja Moinuddin Chishti is located in _____?

                Correct Answer: Ajmer
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • was born in 1143 and died in 1236 AD.
                • He is also known as Gharīb Nawāz (Benefactor of the Poor).
                • He is the most famous Sufi saint of the Chishti order of South Asia.
                • His shrine is at Ajmer, Rajasthan, India.
                319

                Which Article of the Constitution requires that any person arrested shall be produced before a magistrate within 24 hours of the arrest?

                Correct Answer: Article 10(2)
                Show explanation
                Explanation
              • No Detention Beyond 24 Hours: A person cannot be detained for more than 24 hours without the magistrate's authority.
              • Legal Provisions: This rule is enforced under Section 61 of the Criminal Procedure Code (CrPC) and Article 10(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan.
                • 320

                The water dispute between Pakistan and India was ended by signing of which treaty?

                Correct Answer: Indus Water Treaty 1960
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • The Indus Waters Treaty, signed on September 19, 1960, between India and Pakistan and brokered by the World Bank.
                  • It allocates the Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to Pakistan and the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India.
                  • It was signed in Karachi by Pakistan President Ayub Khan, Indian Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru, and Mr. W.A.B. Illif. 
                  • The Treaty became effective on April 1, 1960.
                  321

                  The Line of Control came into existence in _____?

                  Correct Answer: 1972
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Line of Control (LoC) was established in 1949 after the Karachi Agreement, following the first Indo-Pak war over Kashmir.

                  It was originally called the Ceasefire Line and renamed the LoC in the 1972 Simla Agreement.

                  The LoC is approximately 740 km long.

                  322

                  Who was the first woman to be appointed as permanent representative of Pakistan to the United Nations?

                  Correct Answer: Maleeha Lodhi
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Maleeha Lodhi became the first woman Permanent Representative of Pakistan to the United Nations 

                  in office6 February 2015 to 30 October 2019.
                  PresidentMamnoon Hussain Arif Alvi
                  Prime Minster

                  Nawaz Sharif

                  Shahid Khaqan

                  Abbasi Nasirul Mulk (Caretaker)

                  Imran Khan

                  Preceded byMasood Khan
                  Succeeded byOn 30 Sep 2019, she was replaced by Munir Akram, on orders of Prime Minister Imran Khan,


                  323

                  The Wakhan Corridor located to the north of Pakistan belongs to _____?

                  Correct Answer: Afghanistan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Wakhan Corridor is a narrow strip of territory in Afghanistan, extending to China and separating Tajikistan from Pakistan and Kashmir.

                  The corridor was formed in 1893 and created the Durand Line.

                  The Wakhan Corridor is about 350 km long.

                  324

                  When was Lahore High Court established?

                  Correct Answer: 1919
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Established on 21 March 1919.

                  Number of position 60.

                  Judge term length 62 years.

                  There are 5 high courts in Pakistan.

                  325

                  For the Financial Year 2016 - 2017, Netherlands emerged as the second biggest investor in Pakistan in terms of FDI. What was the main reason for that?

                  Correct Answer: They acquired part of ENGRO
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  FrieslandCampina, a Dutch dairy company, acquired a 51% stake in Engro Foods Limited, a Pakistani food and dairy company.

                  This acquisition was the main reason for the Netherlands emerging as the second-largest investor in Pakistan in terms of FDI for the financial year 2016-2017.

                  326

                  Indian spy Kulbushan Jhadav was arrested in _____?

                  Correct Answer: Maskhel
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Indian spy Kulbushan Jadhav was arrested in the Maskhel area of Balochistan, Pakistan, in 2016.

                  He was accused of espionage and involvement in activities against Pakistan's security.

                  327

                  Where was Choudhry Rahmat Ali buried?

                  Correct Answer: UK
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Choudhry Rahmat Ali  is credited with creating the name "Pakistan" in 1933 for a separate Muslim homeland in South Asia.

                  Choudhry Rahmat Ali was buried in 1951 at Cambridge City Cemetery, UK.

                  328

                  Shoaib Akhtar bowled the fastest recorded delivery during the Cricket World Cup in 2003. Against which team Pakistan playing when this happened?

                  Correct Answer: England
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Shoaib Akhtar bowled the fastest recorded delivery of 161.3 km/h during the 2003 Cricket World Cup while playing against England.

                  This delivery remains one of the fastest in the history of cricket.

                  329

                  M-5 Motorway built between Sukkur and Multan is about how many kilometers long?

                  Correct Answer: 392 km
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Sukkur-Multan Motorway is known as M5.

                  It is 392 km long.

                  It running from Multan in the north to Sukkur in the south.

                  Major cities along the M5 include: Jalalpur Pirwala, Shujaabād, and Pano Aqil.

                  The Sukkur Barrage, officially named Lloyd Barrage, is located in Sindh Province.

                  It built from 1923 to 1932 during the British Raj.

                  It was rehabilitated in November 2004. 

                  The barrage is approximately 2 km (1 mi) long.

                  330

                  Borstal Jails or Institutions are meant for _____?

                  Correct Answer: Juvenile Prisoners
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Borstal institutions are designed for juvenile offenders.

                  It focusing on rehabilitation rather than punishment.

                  Pakistan has 1,300 juvenile offenders in jails.

                  92% of them are simply awaiting trial.

                  331

                  The East India Company annexed the Punjab on _____?

                  Correct Answer: 2 April 1849
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The East India Company annexed Punjab on 2 April 1849, following the defeat of the Sikh Empire in the Second Anglo-Sikh War.

                  Punjab became a part of British India, ending Sikh rule under Maharaja Duleep Singh.

                  332

                  From which of the following cities, Maulana Muhammad Ali Jauhar started his English newspaper Comrade in 1911?

                  Correct Answer: Calcutta
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  the famous newspaper "Comrade and Hamdard" was issued Ch Rehmat Ali 


                  Mualana Muhammad Ali Johar issued Comrade English newspaper from Calcutta


                  Maulana launched his famous English weekly “Comrade” from Calcutta in 1911 


                  and his Urdu weekly “Hamdard” from Dehli in 1913.

                  ND5-5-2023



                   

                  333

                  Who was the last Governor General of India under the British East India Company?

                  Correct Answer: Lord Curzon
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Lord Canning was the last Governor-General of East India Company.

                  He became the first Viceroy of India under Crown rule after the Queen Victoria's proclamation (also called Magna Carta of India) in 1858, following the Indian Rebellion of 1857.

                  334

                  Qadianis were declared as Non-Muslims during the government of _____?

                  Correct Answer: Z.A Bhutto
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Qadianis and Ahmadis are considered non-Muslims by many mainstream Muslims.

                  Mirza Ghulam Ahmad founded this movement.

                  In 1974, the Pakistani government enacted the 2nd Amendment to officially declare Ahmadis as non-Muslims.

                  Pakistan is the only country in the world to do so.

                  335

                  Pakistan won ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2009 under the captaincy of which of the following?

                  Correct Answer: Younas Khan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Know about ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2009

                  Edition: Second

                  Hosted by: England

                  Held: 5 June – 21 June 2009

                  Winner: Pakistan

                  Pakistan won: First time

                  Runner-up: Sri Lanka

                  Most wickets taken by: Umar Gul (13 wickets)

                  Pakistani captain: Younas Khan

                   

                  336

                  What is Pakistan's largest trade partner in import?

                  Correct Answer: China
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  China is Pakistan's largest trade partner in imports.

                  The country imports a wide range of goods from China.

                  Including machinery, electronics, and textiles.

                  This making it a key player in Pakistan's trade dynamics.

                  337

                  Gurdwara Rori Sahib is located in Aminabad in which city of Pakistan?

                  Correct Answer: Gujranwala
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Gurdwara Rori Sahib is a gurdwara which is located in Eminabad, Gujranwala, Punjab, Pakistan.

                  It was built during the reign of Maharaja Ranjeet Singh.

                  The gurudwara is a sacred site for Sikhs, built to commemorate the visit of Guru Nanak Dev Ji.

                  338

                  Nanga Parbat is located in _____?

                  Correct Answer: Gilgit
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Nanga Parbat is also locally known as Diamer.
                  • It is part of the Western Himalayas in Gilgit-Baltistan, Pakistan.
                  • The peak, meaning "Naked Mountain," stands at 8,126 m (26,660 ft).
                  • It is the 9th highest mountain globally.
                  • It is also called the "Killer Mountain."
                  • Nanga Parbat was first ascended on July 3, 1953.
                  • Shehroze Kashif recently became the youngest Pakistani to summit it.

                  Rk.MountainHeight(M)Height(F)RangeParent mountainFirst ascentCountry
                  1Mount Everest 8,84829,029Mahalangur Himalayanone1953Nepal
                  2K28,61128,251Baltoro KarakoramMount Everest1954Pakistan, China
                  3Kangchenjunga8,58628,169Kangchenjunga HimalayaMount Everest1955Nepal, Inida
                  4Lhotse8,51627,940Mahalangur HimalayaMount Everest1956Nepal, China
                  5Makalu8,48527,838Mahalangur HimalayaMount Everest1955Nepal, China
                  6Cho Oyu8,18826,864Mahalangur HimalayaMount Everest1954Nepal, China
                  7Dhaulagiri I8,16726,795Dhaulagiri HimalayaK21960Nepal
                  8Manaslu8,16326,781Manaslu HimalayaCho Oyu1956Nepal
                  9Nanga Parbat8,12626,660Nanga Parbat HimalayaDhaulagiri1953Pakistan
                  10Annapurna I8,09126,545Annapurna HimalayaCho Oyu1950Nepal*******

                  VU CBT FIA  PAPER 23-12-2021

                  339

                  The Simon Report was unanimously condemned by all _____?

                  Correct Answer: Political Parties of India
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  In 1930, the Commission published its two-volume report, also known as the Simon Report.

                  ·        The Indian Statutory Commission also known as Simon Commission,

                  ·        was a group of seven Members of Parliament

                  ·        under the chairmanship of Sir John Simon.

                  ·        The commission arrived in India in 1928

                  ·        to study constitutional reform in Britain's largest and most important possession.

                  Simon Commission wanted to check the working of Government of India Act, 1919.


                  340

                  Faisalabad is famous for ______ reason.

                  Correct Answer: Textile
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Faisalabad is formerly known as Lyallpur

                  Faisalabad is the third largest city in Pakistan

                  Faisalabad is the second largest city in the Province of Punjab.

                  Faisalabad is famous for its textile mills

                  Faisalabad is called Manchester of Pakistan because it's the biggest textile city in Pakistan

                  ****

                  ND10-1-2023


                  341

                  Which of the following artificial lakes is present in Rawalakot, AJK?

                  Correct Answer: Banjosa Lake
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Banjosa Lake  is an artificial lake

                  Banjosa Lake is a tourist resort 18 kilometers from the city of Rawalakot in the Poonch District of Azad Kashmir, Pakistan.

                  It is located at an altitude of 1,981 meters.

                  342

                  Name the pact in which the Muslims and Hindus agreed for separate electorate?

                  Correct Answer: Lucknow pact
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Lucknow Pact was an agreement reached between Congress and Muslim League

                  at a joint session of both parties held in Lucknow in December 1916.

                  Through the pact, the two parties agreed to allow representation to religious minorities in the provincial legislatures.

                  343

                  Which of the following is a political dispute between India and Pakistan?

                  Correct Answer: Sir Creek
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Red Line refers to the disputed boundary in the Sir Creek area between Pakistan and India.


                  Additional information:

                  • Sir Creek is located between Pakistan & India
                  • It is located along the southeastern border of the Sindh province of Pakistan and the western border of the Gujarat province in India.
                  • This area is called the Greater Rann of Kutch, a marshland situated along the Arabian Sea.
                  • ***
                  • Sir Creek is important to Pakistan because: It is environmentally important to prevent coastal wetlands from degradation
                  • About 90 km2 of the lake comes under Pakistan, whilst the majority of the lake i.e. 210 km2, lies within India.
                  344

                  How many times has Pakistan won gold medal in Olympic Games?

                  Correct Answer: Four times
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Pakistan has won a total of 04 gold medals in Olympic Games so far

                  Pakistan won its first Olympic Gold Medal in the field of Hockey in Rome held in 1960.

                  Pakistan won its second Olympic Gold Medal in the field of Hockey in Mexico held in 1968.

                  Pakistan won its third Olympic Gold Medal in in the field of Hockey Loss Angels, USA held in 1984.

                  Pakistan won its fourth Olympic Gold Medal in the field of Javelin Throw in Paris held in 2024.

                   *****

                  345

                  Who among the following was convicted in the Rawalpindi Conspiracy Case?

                  Correct Answer: Faiz Ahmad Faiz
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Rawalpindi Conspiracy (also known as the Rawalpindi Conspiracy Case)

                  The Rawalpindi Conspiracy was an attempted coup d'état against the government of Liaquat Ali Khan

                  At  this time Liaquat Ali Khan, the first Prime Minister of Pakistan, in 1951

                  The hearing of the Rawalpindi Conspiracy Case began on the 14th of June 1951 in Hyderabad Jail

                  Faiz Ahmad faiz and Major general Akbar Khan was part of the Rawalpindi Conspiracy Case

                  ND1-5-2023


                  346

                  Hajjaj bin Yousaf demanded compensation for looted ships from which ruler of Sindh?

                  Correct Answer: Raja Dahir
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Raja Dahir's kingdom was invaded by the Arab Umayyad Caliphate, led by Muhammad bin Qasim, in 711 CE.

                  The invasion was partly motivated by the pirate raid off the coast of Debal.

                  It resulted in gifts to the Umayyad caliph from the king of Serendib (modern-day Sri Lanka) being stolen.

                  Raja Dahir was born in 663 CE and ruled Sindh from 695 to 712 CE.

                  He was defeated and killed by Muhammad bin Qasim in the Battle of Aror in 712 CE.

                  347

                  "Attan" is a traditional dance popular in which of the following cultures?

                  Correct Answer: Pashtun
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                • Punjab: Famous for Bhangra dance, Sufi music, and festivals like Basant.
                • Sindh: Known for Ajrak, Sindhi Topi, and Sufi poetry like Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai's work.
                • Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KP): Renowned for Attan dance, Pashtun hospitality, and traditional Peshawari chappals.
                • Balochistan: Famous for traditional crafts, nomadic lifestyle, and Chaap (Balochi sword dance).
                  • 348

                  Molasses is by product of ______?

                  Correct Answer: Sugar
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Molasses, from the Latin word melaceres, meaning honey-like, is a thick dark syrup that is a byproduct of sugar refining.

                  It results when sugar is crystallized out of sugar cane or sugar beet juice.

                  349

                  Who received the coveted Sword of Honour in the first PMA Long Course?

                  Correct Answer: Major Raja Aziz Bhatti
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The highest gallantry award was bestowed upon Maj Raja Aziz Bhatti (Sword of Honour, 1st PMA).

                  Captain Esmond D. Cunha was the first non-Muslim to win the coveted Sword of Honour at the Pakistan Military Academy, Kakul in 1951.

                  350

                  In which month did Misbah-ul-Haq become the first Pakistani to play 50 matches as Test Captain?

                  Correct Answer: November 2016
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Misbah-ul-Haq became the first Pakistani to play 50 matches as Test Captain in November 2016.

                  He made history at Hagley Oval ground in Christchurch during the second day of the Test series against New Zealand.

                  Additional Points:

                  • Misbah-ul-Haq captained Pakistan in 56 Test matches, winning 26, losing 19, and drawing 11.
                  • He is credited with leading Pakistan to the top of the ICC Test rankings in 2016.
                  • Under his captaincy, Pakistan won their first Test series against England at Lord's in 20 years.
                  351

                  Havaldar Lalak Jan Shaheed was awarded Nishan-e-Haider for his acts of bravery and valour during the _____?

                  Correct Answer: Kargil Operation
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Shaheed Lalak Jan was born on April 1, 1967.

                  He was enlisted in the Army as Havaldar on December 10, 1984.

                  He was given country's highest award Nishan-E-Haider for the devotion and courage he showed in the Kargil War in 1999.

                  352

                  Who was the Pakistan's first permanent representative in UNO?

                  Correct Answer: Ahmed Shah Patras Bokhari
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Syed Patras Bokhari was a diplomat who served as a Permanent Representative of Pakistan to the United Nations from 1951 to 1954.

                   

                  353

                  PAL is an organization to look after the welfare of the writers community. PAL stands for ____?

                  Correct Answer: Pakistan Academy of Letters
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  PAL stands for Pakistan Academy of Letters, which promotes the welfare of writers and literary activities in Pakistan.

                  It provides support, recognition, and resources to authors and poets across the country.

                  354

                  When did the Soviet Union official break up?

                  Correct Answer: 26 December 1991
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Soviet Union, officially the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics


                  ****

                  History of the Soviet Union

                  Background

                  1917–1927: Establishment (founded)

                  1927–1953: Stalinism

                  1953–1964: Khrushchev Thaw

                  1964–1982: Era of Stagnation

                  1982–1991: Decline and collapse (dissolved)

                  ******

                  A flagship communist state, it was nominally a federal union of fifteen national republics


                  It was the largest country in the world, covering over 22,402,200 square kilometers (8,649,500 sq mi)


                  spanning eleven time zones.


                  Capital and largest city: Moscow

                  ****

                  Leader  

                  • 1922–1924: Vladimir Lenin

                  • 1924–1953: Joseph Stalin

                  • 1953: Georgy Malenkov

                  • 1953–1964 : Nikita Khrushchev

                  • 1964–1982: Leonid Brezhnev

                  • 1982–1984: Yuri Andropov

                  • 1984–1985: Konstantin Chernenko

                  • 1985–1991: Mikhail Gorbachev

                  ****

                  Head of state  

                  • 1922–1946 (first): Mikhail Kalinin

                  • 1988–1991 (last): Mikhail Gorbachev

                  ***

                  Head of government  

                  • 1922–1924 (first): Vladimir Lenin

                  • 1991 (last): Ivan Silayev


                  ****

                  Currency: Soviet ruble (Rbl) (SUR)

                  ND7-5-2023



                  355

                  President Mamnoon Hussain was born in ______?

                  Correct Answer: Agra, British India
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  Mamnoon Hussain served as the 12th President of Pakistan

                  Mamnoon Hussain was first appointed Governor of Sindh in June 1999 

                  Mamnoon Hussain was appointed Governor by President Rafiq Tarar

                   President of Pakistan (2013–2018)

                  Governor of Sindh (1999–1999)

                  Born: December 23, 1940, Agra, India

                  Hussain was diagnosed with cancer

                  Mamnoon Hussain Died on 14 July 2021 (aged 80) in Karachi, Sindh.

                  Party: Pakistan Muslim League (N)
                  356

                  The 1970 elections in Pakistanis were originally scheduled to be held in October but had to be postponed to December. What was the reason for this postponement?

                  Correct Answer: Floods and Cyclone in East Pakistan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • The Pakistan Peoples Party won a total of 86 seats in the 1970 elections.
                  • 81 of these seats were general seats, and five were women's seats.
                  • All of the Pakistan Peoples Party's seats were won in West Pakistan.
                  • The party did not win any seats in East Pakistan.
                  357

                  In the second Constituent Assembly elected in 1955, the Muslim League formed a coalition government with ____?

                  Correct Answer: United Front
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Muslim League formed a coalition government with the United Front in the second Constituent Assembly elected in 1955.

                  The Muslim League was defeated in the 1955 elections by the United Front, but still managed to form a minority government with the support of the United Front.

                  358

                  Who was the Prime Minister of Pakistan at the time of its rejoining of Commonwealth in 1989?

                  Correct Answer: Benazir Bhutto
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Pakistan left the Commonwealth for the first time in January 1972.

                  It following the recognition of Bangladesh by the Commonwealth.

                  This led to Pakistan's temporary withdrawal before rejoining in 1989.

                  The Commonwealth of Nations is a political association.

                  It is founded by the Parliament of the United Kingdom on December 11, 1931.

                  It's headquarters in London, United Kingdom.

                  359

                  Chaudhry Rehmat Ali who coined the name "Pakistan" died in 1951. Which political position did he hold in the government of Liaquat All Khan?

                  Correct Answer: He held no political position
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Choudhry Rahmat Ali is credited with creating the name "Pakistan" for a separate Muslim homeland in South Asia.

                  Choudhry Rahmat Ali was buried on 3 February 1951 at Cambridge City Cemetery, UK.

                  Rahmat Ali was born on 16 November 1897 into a Punjabi Muslim family. 

                  360

                  Two Pakistani test Cricketers have got out on 199 runs. One of them was Younus Khan who was the other?

                  Correct Answer: Mudassar Nazar
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Mudassar Nazar scored 199 runs against India in 1983, and Younus Khan achieved the same score against India in 2006.

                  361

                  Group Captain Cecil Chaudhry was awarded Sitara-e-Jurat for his acts of volour in 1965 war. After retirement he remained principal of which famous school?

                  Correct Answer: Saint Anthony's School, Lahore
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Cecil Chaudhry was a legendary fighter pilot of Pakistan Air Force.

                  He participated in a number of aerial battles during the 1965 and 1971 Indo-Pak wars.

                  He served as the principal of St Anthonys High School in Lahore and St Marys Academy Lalazar, Rawalpindi.

                  362

                  Under which amendment of the 1973 Constitution, the Objectives Resolution was made a substantive part of the Constitution?

                  Correct Answer: 8th
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Objectives Resolution was made a substantive part of the 1973 Constitution of Pakistan through the Eighth Amendment, which inserted Article 2A into the Constitution.

                  The Objectives Resolution was originally adopted by the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan on March 12, 1949. It was presented by the first Prime Minister of Pakistan, Liaquat Ali Khan, on March 7, 1949.

                  The Objectives Resolution was included in the 1962, 1972 interim, and 1973 constitutions as a preamble.

                  363

                  What was the proportion of Muslim members proposed for the Imperial Legislative Council in the Lucknow Pact?

                  Correct Answer: 1/3rd
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  The Congress-Muslim League signed the Lucknow pact in 1916 which cemented Hindu-Muslim unity to achieve the goal of self-rule. Lucknow Pact suggested the following reforms:
                  • Self-government in India.
                  • Abolition of the Indian Council.
                  • 1/3rd representation to be given to Muslims in the Central Government.
                  • Separation of the executive from the judiciary.
                  • Salaries of the Secretary of State for Indian Affairs to be paid from British coffers and not the Indian funds.
                  • All elected members to be elected directly based on adult franchise. 4/5th of the members of the provincial legislatures to be elected and 1/5th to be nominated.
                  • The number of Muslims in the provincial legislatures to be laid down for each province.
                  • Separate electorates for all communities until a joint electorate is demanded by all.
                  • Introduction of a system of weightage for minority representation.
                  • Increasing the term of the Legislative Council to 5 years.
                  • Half the members of the Imperial Legislative Council to be Indians.
                  • Members of the Legislative Council elect their President themselves.
                  364

                  The name of the province North West Frontier Province was changed to Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, under which amendment of the Constitution?

                  Correct Answer: 18th
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The province of NWFP (North-West Frontier Province) was renamed Khyber Pakhtunkhwa on April 19, 2010.

                  It following the 18th Amendment to the Constitution of Pakistan, with 90 votes in favor.

                  NWFP was established in 1901.

                  At the time of Pakistan's independence in 1947, the Indian National Congress was a significant political party in NWFP.

                  365

                  A.R. Cornelius was a _______?

                  Correct Answer: Chief Justice of Supreme Court
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Alvin Robert Cornelius, H.Pk was a Pakistani jurist, legal philosopher and judge,

                  He served as the 4th Chief Justice of Pakistan from 1960 until 1968

                  He served as Law Minister in the cabinet of Yahya Khan, 1969 – 16 December 1971.

                  Cornelius was born in Agra

                  366

                  The seats are reserved for Non-Muslim in National Assembly are filled _____?

                  Correct Answer: By means of nomination
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Of these, 266 are filled by direct elections.

                  In addition, the Pakistani Constitution reserves 10 seats for religious minorities and 60 seats for women.

                  These are filled by proportional representation among parties with more than 5% of the vote.

                  This is outlined after the 25th Amendment in Article 51.

                  367

                  The President issues an Ordinance, when the Parliament is not in session, on the _____?

                  Correct Answer: Advice of the Prime Minister
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The President can issue an Ordinance when Parliament is not in session, acting on the formal advice of the Prime Minister.

                  368

                  Before becoming Prime Minister of Pakistan, Feroz Khan Noon was the Governor of ____?

                  Correct Answer: East Pakistan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Feroz Khan Noon was the Governor of East Pakistan from 1953 to 1955, before becoming the Prime Minister of Pakistan in 1957.

                  He served as the seventh Prime Minister of Pakistan from December 1957 to October 1958.

                  369

                  The book 'Reconstruction of Religious Thoughts' is written by?

                  Correct Answer: Allama Iqbal
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Reconstruction of Religious Thought in Islam is  delivered by Muhammad Iqbal

                  370

                  In which province is the town Dalbandin located?

                  Correct Answer: Balochistan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Dalbandin is a city in Balochistan province, located near the Afghanistan and Iran border.
                  • The city is famous for its fruit orchards and is surrounded by desert and mountains.
                  • Dalbandin Airport is a small domestic airport located in the southwest of the city.
                  • The city also has a railway station and a football stadium.
                  371

                  In which battle the British forces led by Duke of Wellington defeated French forces led by Napoleon Bonaparte?

                  Correct Answer: Battle of Waterloo
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                   Through a series of wars, he expanded his empire to Western and Central Europe. The Battle of Waterloo, where Napoleon's army was defeated by Britain and Prussia, marked the end of his reign and French rule in Europe.

                  372

                  In the Redcliff 'Award of 1947, the Muslim majority areas of Gurdaspur and Pathankot were handed over to India just because _____?

                  Correct Answer: It provided road link from India to Kashmir valley
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Muslim majority areas of Gurdaspur and Pathankot were handed over to India because they provided a road link from India to the Kashmir valley.

                  This strategic consideration outweighed other factors, resulting in the allocation of these areas to India despite their Muslim majority population.

                  Dar ul Uloom Deoband was established in ________ District.

                  Correct Answer: Saharanpur
                  Show explanation

                    377

                    Which Article of the 1973 Constitution deals with impeachment of the President?

                    Correct Answer: Article 47
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Article 47 of the 1973 Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan deals with the impeachment of the President.

                    Other articles of the 1973 Constitution include:

                    • Article 25-A: Provides free and compulsory education to children aged 5–16 
                    • Article 13: Protects against double punishment and self-incrimination 
                    • Article 22: Safeguards educational institutions in respect of religion 
                    • Article 37 (e): Directs the state to make provisions for securing just and humane conditions of work 
                    • Article 5: Loyalty to the State and obedience to the Constitution and law are the basic duties of every citizen
                    378

                    The Pakistani film "Zarqa" became very popular in Pakistan and the Middle East. Who played the character of Zarqa the movie?

                    Correct Answer: Neelo
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The character of Zarqa was played by Neelo.

                    Neelo was a renowned Pakistani film actress who starred in the 1969 film "Zarqa".

                    379

                    Who gave suggestion for formation of SAARC?

                    Correct Answer: Ziaur Rehman
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    President Ziaur Rahman of Bangladesh proposed the idea of SAARC in 1977.

                    His vision for economic cooperation and cultural exchange among South Asian countries led to the establishment of SAARC in 1985.

                    It consists of eight member states: Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Maldives, Nepal, Pakistan, and Sri Lanka."

                    380

                    Which country paid an official visit by the first Prime Minister of Pakistan in 1950?

                    Correct Answer: America
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The state visit of Liaquat Ali Khan to the United States from 3 May to 5 May 1950.

                    An official state visit paid by the first Prime Minister of Pakistan.

                     Liaquat Ali Khan, accompanied by the First Lady of Pakistan, Ra'ana Liaquat Ali Khan.


                    381

                    To investigate certain offences including corruption federal Investigation Agency-FIA was established in Pakistan by _____?

                    Correct Answer: Prime Minister Z.A Bhutto
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Prime Minister Zulfikar Ali Bhutto established the Federal Investigation Agency (FIA) in Pakistan in 1975.

                    The FIA was established after the promulgation of the FIA Act, 1974 on January 13, 1975.

                    The establishment of the FIA was recommended in a report on "Police Reforms" submitted to the Cabinet by Mr. G. Ahmad in 1972.

                    382

                    How can a person go about renewing his insurance and initiating the process of claims and compensation?

                    Correct Answer: Insurance can be renewed after two years at relevant Proctorate of Emigrant (PE) office or embassy of the host country
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    To renew insurance and initiate the claims and compensation process, the correct procedure is: Insurance can be renewed after two years at the relevant Proctorate of Emigrant (PE) office or the embassy of the host country.

                    This applies to the insurance policies that are related to overseas employment.

                    383

                    Department of Employees' old age benefit institution is dealt under

                    Correct Answer: Labour wing
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Pakistan's Employees' Old Age Benefits Institution (EOBI) operates under the Labour Wing. 

                    It aims to provide financial assistance to workers post-retirement or in case of disability, as well as benefits for their families after the worker's death.

                    384

                    Where would be complaint against advertisement given by a fake recruiting agent be directed?

                    Correct Answer: Federal Investigation Authority
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Federal Investigation Agency (FIA).

                    The FIA handles such cases, including fraudulent advertisements related to overseas employment.

                    The Ministry of Overseas Pakistanis and Human Resources Development was established

                    Correct Answer: 7th June 2013
                    Show explanation

                    MOPHRD was established on June 7, 2013

                    (MOPHRD) stands for The Ministry of Overseas Pakistanis and Human Resource Development

                    Headquarters:: Islamabad

                    390

                    Ali Garh Institute Gazette was published in which language?

                    Correct Answer: English and Urdu
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    It was the first multilingual journal in India, published in 1866 by Sir Syed Ahmed Khan

                    391

                    At the time of partition which state had its own system of currency and postage stamps?

                    Correct Answer: Hyderabad
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Hyderabad was its currency notes after joining the Dominion of India in 1948 and the Republic in 1950.

                    The Hyderabadi rupee was an independent currency separate from the Indian rupee.

                    392

                    The three eastern rivers the Ravi, the Beas and the Sutlej enter Pakistan from:

                    Correct Answer: India
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Ravi, Beas, and Sutlej rivers do not enter Pakistan but are instead given exclusively to India under the Indus Waters Treaty of 1960:

                    • Ravi, Beas, and Sutlej (Given to India)
                    • Indus, Jhelum, and Chenab (Given to Pakistan)
                    Jhelum, Chenab, Ravi, Beas, and Sutlej are tributaries of the Indus River.

                    Panjnad means "five rivers"

                    Above Mithankot, the Indus River receives from Panjnad 

                    Panjnad falls in River Indus

                    The length of Panjnad is: 71 km

                    The Panjnad Rive is located end of the Bahawalpur 

                     Panjnad Headworks has three canals:

                    1. Panjnad canal
                    2. Abbassia canal
                    3. Abbassia link canal

                    393

                    Which one of these architect designed the Quaid-e-Azam tomb?

                    Correct Answer: Yahya Merchant
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
