                    Architect(s): Yahya C. Merchant

                    Construction started: 31 July 1960

                    Inaugurated: 18 January 1971

                    394

                    What was the age of the Qaid-e-Azam at the time of his death?

                    Correct Answer: 71 years 8 month
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah was born on 25th December 1876 in Karachi.

                    He was passed away on 11th September 1948 in Karachi, Pakistan.

                    395

                    Who is the ancient Greek name for River Jhelum?

                    Correct Answer: Hydaspes
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The ancient Greek name for the River Jhelum was Hydaspes.

                    It was historically significant, especially for the Battle of Hydaspes fought between Alexander the Great and King Porus in 326 BCE near this river.

                    396

                    Which one of the following Mountain Passes, has been most frequently used by external invaders?

                    Correct Answer: Khyber pass
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Khyber Pass has been a gateway for invasions of the Indian subcontinent from the northwest for centuries.

                    It has been used by many groups, including the Persians, Greeks, Mughals, Afghans, and the British.

                    The Khyber Pass cuts through the Safed Koh mountains, which are an extension of the Hindu Kush range.

                    It's 1,070 meters high and 140 meters wide.

                    397

                    The facility of GSP Plus, to give a boost to exports from Pakistan, has been given by ______?

                    Correct Answer: EU
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The GSP+ status provided 66% relief on tariffs for Pakistani goods.

                    This allowing easier access to European Union markets by reducing trade barriers.

                    398

                    Which one of the following politicians has served as the 3rd Deputy Prime Minister of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Chaudhry Pervez Elahi
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Ishaq Dar is the 4th Deputy Prime Minister of Pakistan appointed on 28-04-2024

                    List of Deputy Prime Ministers of Pakistan

                    Sr#

                    Name

                    From

                    Till

                    Party

                    1

                    Zulfikar Ali Bhutto

                    7-Dec-71

                    20-Dec-71

                    PPP

                    2

                    Nusrat Bhutto

                    31-Mar-89

                    6-Aug-90

                    PPP

                    3

                    Chaudhry Pervaiz Elahi

                    25-Jun-12

                    16-Mar-13

                    Muslim League (Q)

                    4

                    Ishaq Dar

                    28-Apr-24

                    Incumbent

                    Muslim League (N)

                    399

                    M-9 connects Hyderabad to _____?

                    Correct Answer: Karachi
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Karachi-Hyderabad Motorway (M-9) is a six-lane.

                    It is 136 km long motorway.

                    It connecting the major cities of Karachi and Hyderabad in Sindh province.

                    400

                    Under which amendment of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan (1973), the President was allowed to unilaterally dissolve the National Assembly and elected governments?

                    Correct Answer: 8th Amendment
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The 8th Amendment to the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan (1973) allowed the President to unilaterally dissolve the National Assembly and elected governments.

                    This amendment significantly increased presidential powers during its time.

                    401

                    Who was Prime Minister of Pakistan after Zafarullah Khan Jamali in 2004?

                    Correct Answer: Chaudhry Shujaat Hussain
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    He served as 16th Prime Minister of Pakistan from 30 June 2004 on a temporary basis during a transitional period to accommodate Shaukat Aziz till 28 August 2004.

                    402

                    Who was the first governor of West Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Mushtaq Ahmad Gurmani
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Nawab Mushtaq Ahmed Gurmani was the first Governor of West Pakistan, serving from October 14, 1955 to August 27, 1957.

                    Gurmani was also the last Governor of West Punjab, a province that was abolished in 1955 to create West Pakistan.

                    He was born in 1905.

                    He was died in 1981.

                    403

                    When did Nawaz Sharif become the Chief Minister of Punjab?

                    Correct Answer: 1985
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Nawaz Sharif assumed the office of Chief Minister of Punjab in 1985.

                    • Nawaz Sharif became the 12th prime minister of Pakistan on 1 November 1990, succeeding Benazir Bhutto.
                    • He also became head of IJI.
                    • The Second Nawaz Sharif ministry began on 3 February 1997
                    404

                    Following the establishment of the One Unit, when was the Lahore High Court redesignated as High Court of West Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: 14 October 1955
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Lahore High Court was redesignated as the High Court of West Pakistan on 14 October 1955, following the merger of provinces under the One Unit policy.

                    This marked a shift in administrative and judicial arrangements in West Pakistan.

                    405

                    Of the following, which bowler has the best bowling average (run conceded per wicket)?

                    Correct Answer: Imran Khan
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Imran Khan has the best bowling average of the three bowlers: Wasim Akram, Waqar Younis, and Imran Khan:

                    Imran Khan

                    Between 1971 and 1992, Khan's bowling average was 22.81. In his prime from 1980 to 1988, he took 236 test wickets at an average of 17.77.

                    Waqar Younis

                    Between 1989 and 2003, Younis's bowling average was 23.56 in Tests. In ODIs, his average was 23.84.

                    Wasim Akram

                    Between 1985 and 2002, Akram's bowling average was 23.62 in Tests.

                    406

                    Which country helped Pakistan to build JF-17 Thunder?

                    Correct Answer: China
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The JF-17 was inducted into the PAF in February 2010.

                     It is a fourth-generation aircraft developed jointly by

                    • The Chengdu Aircraft Corporation (CAC) of China
                    • The Pakistan Aeronautical Complex (PAC)

                    Fifty-eight (58) percent of the JF-17 airframe is produced in Pakistan.

                    Forty-two (42) percent is produced in China

                    Manufactured

                    In China: June 2007 – present

                    In Pakistan: January 2008 – present

                    Introduction date: 12 March 2007

                    First flight: 25 August 2003

                    407

                    Sir Gamga Ram in whose name the Ganga Ram hospital exist in Lahore was by education a/an _____?

                    Correct Answer: Engineer
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Sir Ganga Ram Hospital Established by Sir Ganga Ram, a Civil Engineer.

                    He established it in Lahore. In 1921 during the British Raj

                    Sir Ganga Ram Hospital is a hospital in Lahore.


                    408

                    The Muslims League demanded constitutional reforms in India in which year?

                    Correct Answer: 1927
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Muslim League demanded constitutional reforms in India in 1927.

                    However, it was in 1906 that the Muslim League formally began to advocate for separate electorates and reserved seats in the Imperial Council, which were granted in the Indian Councils Act of 1909.

                    409

                    General Raheel Sharif took oath as Chief of Army Staff of Pakistan on which date?

                    Correct Answer: 29 November 2013
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    General Raheel Sharif took oath as the 15th Chief of Army Staff of the Pakistan Army on November 29, 2013.

                    He was appointed by Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif.

                    Sharif retired from the position on November 29, 2016. 

                    Sharif received the Nishan-e-Imtiaz (military) in 2013.

                    He also received five international military awards, which is the most for any Pakistani Army Chief.

                    410

                    Sir Syed was extremely impressed by the education system of which country?

                    Correct Answer: UK
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Sir Syed Ahmad Khan was impressed by the UK's education system.

                    He visited Oxford and Cambridge universities in 1869-70, inspiring him to establish Aligarh Muslim University.

                    411

                    The under construction Gulpur Hydropower Project to generate 102 MW of electricity is located in ________?

                    Correct Answer: Kotli
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Location: Kotli District, Azad Kashmir, Pakistan
                    • Coordinates: 33°27′19″N 73°51′45″E
                    • Distance from Islamabad: About 167 km
                    • Distance from Lahore: About 285 km
                    • River: Poonch River, a tributary of Jhelum River
                    • Dam type: Gravity, roller-compacted concrete
                    • Dam height: 35 m (115 ft)
                    • Dam length: 238 m (781 ft)
                    412

                    Lord Minto came to India as a Viceroy in the Year?

                    Correct Answer: 1905
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    In 1905 Minto was appointed viceroy of India, with John Morley as secretary of state.

                    Lord Minto arrived in India as Viceroy in 1905. He served in this role until 1910. 



                    413

                    In terms of cultivation, which is the largest crop in Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Wheat
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Pakistan's largest food crop is wheat.

                    As of 2018, According to the Ministry of Agriculture, Pakistan's wheat output reached 26.3 million tonnes

                    The most important crops which together account for nearly more than 75% of the value of total crop output are:

                    wheat

                    sugarcane

                    cotton

                    rice

                    ND22-7-2023

                    414

                    Akram Khan Durrani served as the Chief Minister of which province?

                    Correct Answer: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Akram Khan Durrani: remained chief of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (from 2002 to 2007)

                    Akram Khan Durrani: remained federal minister for housing (from 2013 to 2018)

                    415

                    Arya Samaj was against of ________?

                    Correct Answer: All of above
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Arya Samaj was founded in 1875 by Swami Dayanand Saraswati.

                    The Arya Samaj also started the "Shuddhi Movement" in the early 1900s.

                    Some members of the Arya Samaj supported reconverting Muslims and Christians to increase the number of Hindus.

                    416

                    Who has made history by becoming the first Hindu officer in the Police Service of Pakistan (PSP)?

                    Correct Answer: Rajender Meghwar
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Rajender Meghwar became the first Hindu officer in the Police Service of Pakistan (PSP).

                    He was appointed in December 2024, and he began performing his duties in Faisalabad.

                    He belongs to Badin, Sindh.

                    He joined the police force after passing the CSS exams.

                    ****

                    417

                    Who has become the first Hindu PSP officer in the history of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Rajender Meghwar
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Rajender Meghwar became the first Hindu officer in the Police Service of Pakistan (PSP).

                    He was appointed in December 2024, he began performing his duties in Faisalabad.

                    He belongs to Badin, Sindh.

                    He joined the police force after passing the CSS exams.

                    ****

                    418

                    اُردو افسانہ گڈریا کس کی تخلیق ہے ؟

                    Correct Answer: اشفاق احمد
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    "گڈریا" اردو افسانہ اشفاق احمد کی تخلیق ہے۔
                    اشفاق احمد اردو ادب کے معروف افسانہ نگار، ڈرامہ نگار، اور مصنف تھے۔
                    "گڈریا" ان کے مشہور افسانوں میں شامل ہے

                    419

                    علامہ اقبال نے اپنے مجموعہ پیام مشرق کو کس نام سے منسوب کیا ؟

                    Correct Answer: امیر امان اللہ خان
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    علامہ محمد اقبال نے اپنے مجموعہ "پیام مشرق" کو امیر امان اللہ خان کے نام سے منسوب کیا تھا۔

                    420

                    علامہ محمد اقبال کے پی۔ ایچ۔ ڈی کے مقالہ کو پہلی مرتبہ انگلستان میں کس سال شائع کیا گیا ؟

                    Correct Answer: 1908
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    علامہ محمد اقبال نے"شاعر فارس کے کاموں میں فارسی شاعری" کے عنوان سے اپنے پی ایچ ڈی کا مقالہ 1908 میں کیمبرج یونیورسٹی سے مکمل کیا تھا
                    اور یہ مقالہ پہلی بار انگلستان میں 1908 میں شائع ہوا۔
                    اس مقالے میں اقبال نے فارسی شاعری کی اہمیت اور اس کے مختلف پہلوؤں کو اجاگر کیا تھا۔ اقبال کا یہ مقالہ ان کی علمی قابلیت اور شاعری کے عمیق تجزیہ کا مظہر ہے، اور یہ ان کے فکری ارتقا کا ایک اہم سنگ میل تھا۔

                    421

                    مشہور زمانہ کردار میاں خوجی کس کی تخلیق ہے ؟

                    Correct Answer: رتن ناتھ سرشار
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    میاں خوجی ایک مشہور کردار ہے جس کی تخلیق رتن ناتھ سرشار نے کی۔
                    رتن ناتھ سرشار کا شمار اردو ادب کے اہم مصنفین میں ہوتا ہے، اور ان کا سب سے مشہور کام "فسانۂ عجائب" ہے۔

                    422

                    سترہ سو تئیسء میں آگرہ میں کون سے شاعر پیدا ہوئے ؟

                    Correct Answer: میر تقی میر
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    میر تقی میر ۱۷۲۳ء میں آگرہ میں پیدا ہوئے اور وہ اردو شاعری کے مشہور اور عظیم شاعروں میں شمار ہوتے ہیں۔

                    ان کی شاعری میں عاطفی گہرائی اور سادگی کے ساتھ ساتھ ان کی زبان کی سلیسیت اور روانی نے انہیں اردو ادب میں منفرد مقام دلایا۔

                    423

                    شیر علی افسوس نے باغ اردو نام سے ترجمہ کس مشہور فارسی کتاب کا کیا؟

                    Correct Answer: گلستان سعدی
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    شیر علی افسوس نے "باغ اردو" کے نام سے "گلستان سعدی" کا اردو میں ترجمہ کیا۔
                    گلستان سعدی  ایک مشہور فارسی کتاب ہے جسے سعدی شیرازی نے لکھا تھا۔

                    424

                    انار کلی ڈرامہ کس کی تصنیف ہے؟

                    Correct Answer: سید امتیاز علی تاج
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    انار کلی ایک مشہور اردو ڈرامہ ہے جو 1922 میں سید امتیاز علی تاج نے لکھا۔

                    یہ ڈرامہ تاریخی موضوع پر مبنی ہے اور مغل شہزادہ سلطان سلیم (جو بعد میں جہانگیر کے نام سے مشہور ہوئے) اور انار کلی کی محبت کی کہانی پر مبنی ہے

                    425

                    وہ کون سے شاعر تھے جو 1911 ء میں سیالکوٹ میں پیدا ہوئے اور لاہور میں وفات پائی؟

                    Correct Answer: فیض احمد فیض
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    فیض احمد فیض 13 فروری 1911ء کو سیالکوٹ میں پیدا ہوئے
                    اور 20 نومبر 1984ء کو لاہور میں وفات پا گئے
                    426

                    Pakistan Mineral Development Corporation (PMDC) was established in the year _____?

                    Correct Answer: 1974
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    PMDC is an autonomous corporation under the administrative control of Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Resources.

                    PMDC founded in 1974.

                    427

                    How many megawatts of electricity is being generated from the Ghazi Brotha project?

                    Correct Answer: 1450 megawatt
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Ghazi-Barotha Hydropower Project is a 1,450 MW un-of-the-river hydropower, connected to the Indus River.

                    Ghazi-Barotha  Location Between Attock, Punjab and Haripur, KP

                    Construction of the project began in 1995. 

                    Opening date: 2002

                    The Official name of Ghazi-Barotha Hydropower Project is غازى بروتھا

                    428

                    The Rabi season in Pakistan lasts from _____?

                    Correct Answer: November to April
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Rabi season in Pakistan lasts from November–December to April–May.

                    This is one of the two main crop seasons in Pakistan, along with the Kharif season.

                    Kharif season: The sowing season for Kharif crops begins in April–June, and the harvesting season is October–December. 

                    Rabi crops: Examples of Rabi crops include barley, gram, peas, and wheat. 

                    Kharif crops: Examples of Kharif crops include cotton, groundnut, maize, and rice.

                    429

                    Which is the oldest seaport of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Port Qasim
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Pakistan has three big sea ports named as Karachi Port, Muhammad Bin Qasim port, and Gwadar port.

                    Port of Muhammad Bin Qasim is the oldest port while the Karachi port is the busiest one.

                    430

                    شبلی نعمانی نے کس ادارے کی بنیاد رکھی؟

                    Correct Answer: دارا المصنفین
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation


                    دار المصنفین شبلی اکیڈمی ایک علمی و تحقیقی ادارہ ہے جو بھارت کے شہر اعظم گڑھ میں واقع ہے۔

                    اس ادارے کا قیام علامہ شبلی نعمانی کا دیرینہ خواب تھا

                    دارالمصنفین کے قیام کی بات علامہ شبلی نے سب سے پہلے 1910ء میں ندوۃ العلما کے اجلاس میں کی تھی۔

                    شبلی نے اپنی موروثی جائداد میں 

                    431

                    Tomb of Nur Jahan is located in __________?

                    Correct Answer: Lahore
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Tomb of Nur Jahan is a 17th-century mausoleum in Lahore

                    Nur Jahan, born on 1577

                    Died on – 18 December 1645 

                    Noor Jehan was the wife and chief consort of the Mughal emperor Jahangir 

                    She achieved unprecedented political power for a woman in Mughal India

                    ****

                    also Remember it

                    Noor Jahan Singer's Resting place is Gizri Graveyard, Karachi, Sindh.

                    432

                    Siyassat-Namah was composed by whom?

                    Correct Answer: Nizam-ul-Mulk
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Siyassat-Namah (The Book of Governance) was written by Nizam-ul-Mulk,

                    433

                    The British government accepted to Muslim League Demand for Pakistan on?

                    Correct Answer: 3rd June 1947
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The British government announced the Mountbatten Plan on June 3, 1947

                    The 3rd June Plan announced the partition of British India into 2 states:

                    • India
                    • Pakistan


                    Punjab and Bengal to be divided according to 3rd June Plan

                    This Plan also known as the Mountbatten Plan or Partition Plan 

                    It was the last plan for independence.

                    434

                    On behalf of the Muslim League, Quaid i Azam signed the Lucknow Pact, who signed it on, behalf of the Congress?

                    Correct Answer: None of them
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Bal Gangadhar Tilak represented the Congress while framing the deal, and Muhammad Ali Jinnah (who joined the Muslim League in 1913)

                    • The Congress League Pact, also known as the Lucknow Pact, was signed in December 1916 at Lucknow by the Indian National Congress and the All-India Muslim League (AIML).
                    • The Congress adopted the Lucknow Pact on December 29, 1916, and by the AIML on December 31, 1916.

                    435

                    Pakistan Council of Scientific & Industrial Research (PCSIR) was established in

                    Correct Answer: 1953
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Pakistan Council of Scientific & Industrial Research (PCSIR) was established in Karachi in 1953.

                    It has also established laboratories in Lahore, Peshawar, and Sialkot.

                    Its head office is located in Islamabad

                    Since 1973, it is functioning under the Act of Parliament, which was amended in 1984.

                    436

                    Mr. I.I. Chundrigar was the Prime Minister of Pakistan from:

                    Correct Answer: Oct 18, 1957 to December 16, 1957
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Chundrigar served the third-shortest term of any Prime Minister in Pakistan: 17 October 1957 – 11 December 1957, 55 days into his term.

                    He was born in Midnapore, Ahmadabad, on 15th April 1898

                    He was the fifth Prime Minister 


                    437

                    In 1946 the mission sent by British Govt. was called

                    Correct Answer: Cabinet Mission
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation


                    The mission sent by the British government to India in 1946 was called the Cabinet Mission:

                    The Cabinet Mission of 1946 consisted of three members:

                    1. Lord Pethick-Lawrence: The Secretary of State for India
                    2. Sir Stafford Cripps: The President of the Board of Trade
                    3. A.V. Alexander: The First Lord of the Admiralty
                    438

                    Which woman was part of the delegation first sent to UNO after the creation of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Shaista Ikramuliah
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                     Begum Shaista Ikramullah was the first female representative in the first Constituent Assembly of Pakistan, which was established in 1947

                    439

                    Which Bank gave a Rs. 80 million loan to the Government of Pakistan on the request of Quaid-e-Azam when the Reserve Bank of India failed to deliver Pakistan's share?

                    Correct Answer: Habib Bank Limited
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    HBL Bank gave a loan to Pakistan for its first budget

                    ***

                    HBL was the first commercial bank to be established in Pakistan in 1947

                    ***

                    HBL moved its operations to Pakistan in 1947 at the request of Muhammad Ali Jinnah, hence becoming the first commercial bank to lay its foundation in the country

                    440

                    The search for suitable words of National Anthem with the music set by A.G. Chagla finally ended with the approval on 13th August?

                    Correct Answer: 1954
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The National Anthem was officially adopted on 13 August 1954.

                    It was written by Hafeez Jalandhari in 1952 

                    The music was produced by Ahmad G. Chagla in 1949.

                    Ahmed Rushdi recorded the National Anthem of Pakistan in 1954.

                    441

                    Govt. of Pakistan constituted a Committee under the chairmanship of Sardar Abdur Rab Nishtar for selecting National anthem in December?

                    Correct Answer: 1948
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Sardar Abdur Rab Nishtar chaired the National Anthem Committee of Pakistan in December 1948

                    The NAC's task was to create the lyrics and composition for Pakistan's national anthem.

                    The NAC eventually selected a melody by :

                    • Composer Ahmad G. Chagla
                    • Lyrics by Hafeez Jalandhari

                    The anthem was first played on Radio Pakistan on August 13, 1954.

                    442

                    Which hypersonic missile was tested in 2024 by Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Fatah-II
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Pakistan tested the hypersonic missile Fatah-II in 2024.

                    This test showcased advancements in its defense capabilities.

                    443

                    The salt range is located in the _____ section of the Indus plain.

                    Correct Answer: North-Western
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Salt Range is situated in the North-Western section of the Indus plain in Pakistan, spanning parts of Punjab.

                    The highest peak of salt range is Soan Sakaser

                    The Salt Range is a mountain range in the north of the Punjab province of Pakistan

                    The range extends along the south of the Potohar Plateau and the north of the Jhelum River.

                    The salt range is situated between rivers Soan and River Jhelum

                    Sakesar (Khushab District) is the highest mountain in the salt region
                    675 meters is the average height of the salt range

                    The Upper Indus plain area is located in the South of the Salt Range and east of Sulemania

                    ******

                    ND17-7-2023

                    444

                    Broghil Pass connects Pakistan with?

                    Correct Answer: Afghanistan
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Broghil Pass connects Pakistan with Afghanistan.
                    • Broghol Pass is a high mountain pass that crosses the Hindu Kush mountain range and connects Wakhan District of Badakhshan Province in Afghanistan with Chitral District of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa in Pakistan.
                    445

                    Which of the following is the most important feature of the Government of India Act of 1935?

                    Correct Answer: Provinces were made separate legal entities
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The most important feature of the Government of India Act of 1935 was the grant of a large degree of autonomy to the provinces of India.

                    Here are some other important features of the Government of India Act of 1935:

                    • Direct elections
                    • Provincial autonomy
                    • Responsible governments
                    • Federation of India
                    • Federal Court
                    • Separation of Burma
                    446

                    Khabikki Lake is in which district?

                    Correct Answer: Khushab
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Khabikki Lake is a saltwater lake.

                    It is located in the Soan Sakaser Valley in the southern Salt Range.

                    The lake is situated in Khushab District, Punjab, Pakistan.

                    447

                    Pakistan's Second Nuclear Power Plant was set up at ____?

                    Correct Answer: Chashma
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Pakistan's first nuclear power plant was set up at Karachi, specifically the Karachi Nuclear Power Plant (KANUPP), which was commissioned on November 28, 1972.

                    The second nuclear power plant in Pakistan was set up at Chashma, in the Punjab province.

                    The Chashma Nuclear Power Plant (CNPP) Unit 1, also known as C-1, was commissioned on September 14, 2000.

                    448

                    When was the local government system under the devolution of power plan, 2001, inaugurated?

                    Correct Answer: Aug 14, 2001
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Devolution is the statutory delegation of powers from the central government of a sovereign state to govern at a sub national level, such as a regional or local level.

                    It is a form of administrative decentralization.

                    449

                    The Karakoram is a word of which language?

                    Correct Answer: Turkish word
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The word Karakoram is a Turkic term that means "black gravel".
                    • The Karakoram Highway (KKH) is also known as the Friendship Highway in China and N-35 in Pakistan.
                      • It was built by the governments of Pakistan and China and completed in 1979.
                      • Starting from Hassan Abdal, it spans 1,300 (810 miles) km in Pakistan.
                      • The Silk Route is also referred to as the Karakoram Highway.
                      • The Karakoram Range, located between China and Pakistan.
                      • It is traversed by the Karakoram Highway.
                      • It connects the two countries via the Khunjerab Pass.
                      450

                      Hamal lake is located in _____?

                      Correct Answer: Qambar Shahdadkot
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Hamal Lake is situated in Qambar Shahdadkot District in Sindh, Pakistan, 58 km from Larkana city and 40 km from Qambar town.


                      451

                      The 24th Amendment in the Constitution (1973) is about _____?

                      Correct Answer: Right to Protection of Property.
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Article 24 of the Constitution of Pakistan (1973) guarantees the fundamental right to acquire, hold, and dispose of property for all citizens.

                      452

                      Mangla dam is located in _____?

                      Correct Answer: AJ&K
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The Mangla Dam in the Mirpur District of Azad Kashmir, Pakistan

                      It is situated on the Jhelum River 

                      It is the sixth-largest dam in the world.

                      It was constructed with the help of the World Bank 

                      Mangra Dam is named after the nearby village of Mangra.

                      Its Construction was started in 1961 and completed in 1967.

                      It has 5 tunnels and a power station

                      ND17-7-2023 

                      453

                      Mir Shahdad Jo Qubo (Tomb of Mir Shahdad Talpur) is in ______?

                      Correct Answer: Shahpur Chakar
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Mir Shahdad Jo Qubo, Tomb of Mir Shahdad Talpur, is located in Shahpur Chakar city of Sanghar District, in Sindh.

                      The Tomb is one of the historical heritages of Sindh.

                      This is a graveyard of the family members of Mir Shahdad Talpur.

                      Mir Shahdad Talpur is regarded as one of the finest military commanders of Sindh. Mir Shahdad Khan Talpur and Mir Chakar Khan Talpur are well known 12th century generals of the Kalhora Dynasty in Sindh.

                      454

                      When Karachi was made the capital of Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: 12th March 1948
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Karachi was officially declared the capital of Pakistan on 12th March 1948.

                      Karachi remained capital of Pakistan from 1947 to 1959.

                      Rawalpindi remained capital of Pakistan from 1959 to 1967.

                      Islamabad is the current capital of Pakistan since 14 August 1967.

                      455

                      First Governor-General of Pakistan fixed his salary as?

                      Correct Answer: Rs 1
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      List of Governors-General of Pakistan
                      Term Name Birth–Death Monarch Took office Left office Political party Remarks
                      1 Muhammad Ali Jinnah (1876–1948) George VI
                      (1947–1952)                      		 15-Aug-47 11-Sep-48 Pakistan Muslim League died in office
                      2 Sir Khawaja Nazimuddin (1894–1964) = 14-Sep-48 17-Oct-51 Pakistan Muslim League became prime minister

                      4 Iskander Mirza (1899–1969) Elizabeth II
                      (1952–1956)                      		 7-Aug-55 23-Mar-56 Republican Party became president
                      456

                      Which barrage is not on the Indus River?

                      Correct Answer: Rasul Barrage
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Six barrages on the Indus River

                      1. Guddu Barrage
                      2. Sukkur Barrage
                      3. Kotri Barrage 
                      4. Taunsa Barrage
                      5. Chashma Barrage
                      6. Jinnah Barrage.
                      457

                      In which year third round table conference held?

                      Correct Answer: 1932
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • In 1932 third round table conference held
                      • ***
                        First round table conference: 1930
                      • 2nd round table conference: 1931
                      • 3rd round table conference:1932
                      • All round table conference held in London
                      • ***
                      • The three Round Table Conferences of 1930–32 were a series of conferences to formulate the future constitution of India in the light of suggestions given by the Indian Leaders
                      • ***
                      • Gandhi a leader does not attend the first round table conference 

                      458

                      Which of the following Round Table Conference was held in 1932?

                      Correct Answer: Third
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • The first Round Table Conference convened from 12 November 1930 to 19 January 1931
                      • ****
                      • The Second Round table conference was held from 7 September 1931 to 1 December 1931 in London. 
                      • ****
                      • The Third Round Table Conference was held in 1932 between November and December. 
                      • ****

                      • First round table conference: 1930

                        2nd: 1931

                        3rd:1932

                        All in London

                      • All three round table conferences were attended by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar and Tej Bahadur Sapru.
                      459

                      Which of the following Round Table Conference was held in 1931?

                      Correct Answer: Second
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • The first Round Table Conference convened from 12 November 1930 to 19 January 1931
                      • ****
                      • The Second Round table conference was held from 7 September 1931 to 1 December 1931 in London. 
                      • ****
                      • The Third Round Table Conference was held in 1932 between November and December. 
                      • ****

                      • First round table conference: 1930

                        2nd: 1931

                        3rd:1932

                        All in London

                      • All three round table conferences were attended by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar and Tej Bahadur Sapru.
                      460

                      Which of the following Round Table Conference was held in 1930?

                      Correct Answer: First
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • The first Round Table Conference convened from 12 November 1930 to 19 January 1931
                      • ****
                      • The Second Round table conference was held from 7 September 1931 to 1 December 1931 in London. 
                      • ****
                      • The Third Round Table Conference was held in 1932 between November and December. 
                      • ****

                      • First round table conference: 1930

                        2nd: 1931

                        3rd:1932

                        All in London

                      • All three round table conferences were attended by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar and Tej Bahadur Sapru.
                      461

                      How many round table conferences held in 1930 -32?

                      Correct Answer: 3
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      There are three Round table conferences, all held in London

                      • The first Round Table Conference held from 12 November 1930 to 19 January 1931
                      • The Second Round Table conference was held from 7 September 1931 to 1 December 1931 in London. 
                      • The Third Round Table Conference was held in 1932 between November and December. 
                      462

                      How many Round Table Conferences were held in London to resolve the Hind-Muslim differences?

                      Correct Answer: 3
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • The first Round Table Conference convened from 12 November 1930 to 19 January 1931

                      • ****
                      • The Second Round table conference was held from 7 September 1931 to 1 December 1931 in London. 
                      • ****
                      • The Third Round Table Conference was held in 1932 between November and December. 

                      • ****

                      • First round table conference: 1930

                        2nd: 1931

                        3rd:1932

                        All in London

                      • All three round table conferences were attended by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar and Tej Bahadur Sapru.

                      ***

                      ND28-4-2023


                      463

                      The Round Table Conference was held in which city?

                      Correct Answer: London
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      In response to the inadequacy of the Simon Report, the Labour Government under Ramsay MacDonald decided to hold Round Table Conferences in London.

                      The first conference was held from November 12, 1930, to January 19, 1931, to discuss constitutional reforms in India.

                      464

                      Correct Answer: Hussain Shaheed Suharwardi

                      465

                      The first Pakistani Cricketer to score a double century in ODI is?

                      Correct Answer: Fakhar Zaman
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Fakhar Zaman is the first Pakistani cricket player to score a double century in ODIs.

                      He made 210 runs off 156 balls against Zimbabwe (4th ODI at Bulawayo in 2108)

                      He becomes the sixth international batsman to make double-hundreds in ODIs.





                      466

                      What was the full name of Prime Minister I.I. Chundrigar?

                      Correct Answer: Ibrahim Ismail Chundrigar
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Ibrahim Ismail Chundrigar (15 September 1897 – 26 September 1960) was a Pakistani politician who served as the sixth prime minister of Pakistan, appointed in this capacity on 17 October 1957.

                      467

                      River Kunhar flow through Kaghan Valley and joins which river?

                      Correct Answer: Jhelum
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      River Kunhar flows through the Kaghan Valley and joins the Jhelum River.

                      Specifically, the confluence of the two rivers occurs just outside Muzaffarabad, in the Azad Jammu and Kashmir province, Pakistan.

                      Basin:

                      • The Kunar River is part of the Indus River watershed basin. 

                      Other names:

                      • The Kunhar River is also known as Nain-Sukh, which means "eye's repose".
                      468

                      Name the Shaheed belonging to Gilgit-Baltistan, who was awarded Nishan-e-Haider for his extraordinary gallantry and courage?

                      Correct Answer: Hawaldar Lalak Jan
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Shaheed Lalak Jan was born on April 1, 1967.

                      He was enlisted in the Army as Havaldar on December 10, 1984.

                      He was given country's highest award Nishan-E-Haider for the devotion and courage he showed in the Kargil War in 1999.

                      469

                      Bukkur (Bakhar) Fort is located in the province of _______?

                      Correct Answer: Sindh
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Bukkur Fort is an island located in Rohri, Sukkur District of Sindh 

                      Bukkur Fort Built in 1225 A.D.

                      in the seventh century Hijri Sayyid Muhammad Al-Makki Named Bukkur (Dawn)

                      Nowadays Bukkur island is occupied by an Army Public School

                      470

                      Harappa is situated along the banks of which river?

                      Correct Answer: Ravi
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Harappa is situated along the banks of the Ravi River.

                      It is located in the Sahiwal district Punjab province of Pakistan.

                      The Harappan Civilization is also called as Indus Valley Civilization.

                      471

                      The system of Dyarchy was introduced at the Provisional level by the _____?

                      Correct Answer: Montagu - Chelmsford Reforms
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The Montagu-Chelmsford Reforms were a series of constitutional reforms introduced by the British government through the Government of India Act of 1919.

                      The reforms were led by Edwin Samuel Montagu, the secretary of state for India from 1917–22, and Lord Chelmsford, the viceroy of India from 1916–21.

                      472

                      Congress agreed to reserve _____ seats for Muslims in the Central Legislative Council while giving consent to the Lucknow Pact at a joint session of Muslim League and Congress held at Lucknow in December 1916.

                      Correct Answer: One Third
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      In the Lucknow Pact of 1916, Congress agreed to reserve one-third seats for Muslims in the Central Legislative Council.

                      This agreement, formulated during a joint session of the All-India Muslim League and Congress, aimed to foster Hindu-Muslim unity against British rule.

                      473

                      The main demand of simla Muslim Deputation accepted in Minto Morley Reforms of 1906 was about?

                      Correct Answer: Separate electorates
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Simla Deputation organized by the Indian Muslim leaders met the Governor-General and Viceroy Lord Minto in Simla on 1 October 1906. 

                      The delegation of Simla deputation met Lord Minto on 1st October 1906

                      Simla's deputation met with لارڈ منٹو

                      شملہ وفد 1906

                      1. شملہ وفد یکم اکتوبر 1906 کو وائسرائے ہند سے ملا.

                      2. شملہ وفد کا صدر سر آغا خان تھا.

                      3. اس میں 35 لوگ / ارکان شامل تھے.

                      4. سب سے پہلے شملہ وفد ایم اے علی گڑھ کالج کے پرنسپل سے ملا.

                      5. پر. نسپل نے سیکرٹری وائسرائے کرنل ڈونلپ سے یہ میٹنگ طے کر دیا، ان ہی کےذریعے وفد وائسرائے لارڈ منٹو سے ملا.

                      6. چونکہ مسلمانوں کا یہ وفد وائسرائے ہند سے شملہ کے مقام پر ملا، اس وجہ سے اسکا نام شملہ وفد بن گیا.


                      474

                      Indian Council Act 1861 established Legislative Council in the Presidencies of _____?

                      Correct Answer: Bombay and Madras
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The Indian Council Act of 1861 established legislative councils in the Presidencies of Bombay and Madras.

                      These presidencies were given the power to make laws for their respective regions.

                      The act restored the legislative powers that had been taken away by the Charter Act of 1833.

                      475

                      Article ______ of Pakistan's Constitution says that Islam should be the State Religion.

                      Correct Answer: 2
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Article 2 of the Constitution says that Islam shall be the State Religion of Pakistan. 


                      Additional information:

                      Article 3 of the Constitution of Malaysia 1957 declares that Islam is a religion of the federation

                      Article 12 of the Constitution says that:

                      the official religion is Islam and the twelve Jaffrey Schools in Asul-al-Din and Fiqh will remain eternally immutable.

                      476

                      Muslim League opposed Khilafat Movement because _____?

                      Correct Answer: All of these
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The Muslim League opposed the Khilafat Movement for the following reasons:

                      1. Dissention among Muslims: The movement caused divisions within the Muslim community.
                      2. Preference for secular and constitutional approaches: The Muslim League favored political approaches that were more secular and based on constitutional methods.
                      3. Alignment with Gandhi's Non-Cooperation Movement: This association was seen as potentially leading to chaos and disorder, as the objectives and methods of the two movements were not fully aligned.
                      477

                      The Shalimar Garden located in Lahore, Pakistan is the architectural artifacts of which period?

                      Correct Answer: Mughals
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan built Shalimar Gardens in Lahore.

                      Its construction was started in the year 1647 and completed in 1959

                      It is included in World Heritage Site: 1981

                      Its also known as the Jamia Masjid of Thatta



                      Sr#

                      Built by

                      Name of Places

                      1

                      Jahangir​

                      Shalimar Bagh, Kashmir

                      2

                      Shah Jahan

                      1. Shalimar Garden, Pakistan,

                       

                      2. Taj Mahal,

                      3. the Jama Masjid,

                      4. the Red Fort

                      3

                      Akbar

                      1.    Buland Darwaza,

                      2.    Fatehpur Sikri

                      4

                      Aurangzeb

                      Moti Masjid, Delhi


                      478

                      Sindhi Culture Day is celebrated on first Sunday of which month?

                      Correct Answer: December
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Punjab Culture Day is celebrated on 14 March.

                      Balochi Culture Day is celebrated on March 2.

                      Sindhi Culture Day is celebrated on first Sunday of December.

                      Pashtun Culture Day is celebrated on September 23.

                      479

                      Which mosque is located inside Lahore Fort?

                      Correct Answer: Moti Masjid
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The Moti Masjid (Pearl Mosque) is located inside the Lahore Fort.

                      It was built by Emperor Aurangzeb in the 17th century 
                      It is known for its elegant white marble structure.

                      480

                      Nawab Jahangir Yar was from which princely state?

                      Correct Answer: Junagarh
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Nawab Jahangir Yar was from the princely state of Junagarh, located in present-day Gujarat, India.

                      Muhammad Jahangir Khanji was the Nawab of Junagarh from 1989–2023

                      Known for: Struggle for the independence of Junagarh state

                      Khanji died of cancer in Karachi on 20 July 2023, at the age of 67

                      481

                      Which female teacher received the Nobel Prize?

                      Correct Answer: Malala Yousafzai
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Malala Yousafzai was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 2014 at the age of 17 for her advocacy of girls' education

                      In October 2014, Malala, along with Indian children's rights activist Kailash Satyarthi 

                      About Malala Yousafzai

                      Born: 1997

                      Belongs: Mingora, Swat

                      Received Noble Prize in 2014

                      She is the world's youngest Nobel Prize laureate

                      International Malala Day is observed every year on July 12 

                      482

                      Rasul Barrage is located on which river?

                      Correct Answer: Jhelum River
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Rasul Barrage is a barrage on the River Jehlum

                      Between Jhelum District and Mandi Bahauddin District of the Punjab.

                      It is situated 72 km downstream of Mangla Dam.

                      The current structure of the barrage was constructed in 1968

                      483

                      Who was the major supporter of the Two-Nation Theory?

                      Correct Answer: Allama Iqbal
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • The Allahabad Address was a notable speech by Sir Muhammad Iqbal.
                        • He delivered during the 25th annual session of the All-India Muslim League on 29 December 1930, in Allahabad, British India.
                        • Dr. Sir Muhammad Iqbal presided over this session.
                        484

                        میں برطانیہ نے ہندوستان سے متعلق کون سا دستاویز پیش کیا؟ 1933

                        Correct Answer: وائٹ پیپر
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        1933 میں برطانیہ نے ہندوستان کے آئینی اصلاحات کے لیے "وائٹ پیپر" پیش کیا، جو بعد میں گورنمنٹ آف انڈیا ایکٹ 1935 کی بنیاد بنا

                        485

                        What is the tenure of Pakistan's National Security Policy(NSP)?

                        Correct Answer: 5 years
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        The policy document is meant for five years (2022-26)

                        The Public version of the NSP was launched on 14 January 2022 

                        NSP was approved by the federal cabinet on Dec 28, 2021.

                        Prime Minister Imran Khan reveals the country's first-ever NSP

                        486

                        Al-Farodoisi was a poet of which language?

                        Correct Answer: Persian
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        He was a Persian poet and the author of Shahnameh ("Book of Kings")

                        He  is one of the world's longest epic poems created by a single poet

                        Born: 940 Tus, Iran, Samanid Empire

                        Died: 1019 or 1025 (79 or 85 years old) Tus, Iran, Ghaznavid Empire

                        487

                        What was the year of the last Mysore War?

                        Correct Answer: 1799
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        The last Mysore War (also known as the Fourth Anglo-Mysore War) took place in 1799

                        The Fourth Anglo-Mysore War began in 1798 between the British East India Company and the Kingdom of Mysore, led by Tipu Sultan.

                        It concluded in 1799 with the defeat of Mysore and the death of Tipu Sultan.

                        488

                        When Pak purchased nuclear program from France?

                        Correct Answer: March 1976
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        In March 1976, Pakistan and France signed an agreement for a plutonium-based reprocessing plant at Chasma

                        The deal included a 600 MW power plant 

                        France canceled the deal in August 1978


                        Additional information:

                        Pakistan began developing nuclear weapons in 1972 under Prime Minister Zulfikar Ali Bhutto.

                        Pakistan conducted nuclear tests in 1998 and is now considered a nuclear weapon state.

                        489

                        Pakistan’s Coronavirus vaccine campaign started from _____?

                        Correct Answer: 2 February 2021
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Pakistan's COVID-19 vaccine drive began on Tuesday, starting with frontline healthcare workers.

                        Prime Minister Imran Khan attended the launch, where a doctor received the first Sinopharm shot.

                        China donated 500,000 doses of Sinopharm as a goodwill gesture to assist Pakistan.

                        490

                        The book Khutbat-e-Ahmadiya is written by______?

                        Correct Answer: Sir Syed Ahmad Khan
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Khutbat-e-Ahmadiya published in 1870.

                        Khutbat-e-Ahmadiya written by Sir Syed Ahmed Khan. 
                         


                        491

                        گھر سے گھر تک کس کی تحریر ہے؟

                        Correct Answer: احمد ندیم قاسمی
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        گھر سے گھر تک اور کپاس کا پھول اردو کے شاہکار افسانے ہیں۔

                        افسانہ نگار احمد ندیم قاسمی ہے

                        Publication details: لاہور : سنگ میل پبلیشر ، ٢٠١٥

                        خلاصہ: ایک ممتاز پاکستانی افسانہ نگار اور شاعر کی مختصر کہانیاں۔

                        492

                        Who wrote the book "Muslim Response to the West: Muslim Historiography in India 1857-1914"?

                        Correct Answer: Muhammad Aslam Sayed
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Muhammad Aslam Syed wrote Muslim Response to the West: Muslim Historiography in India, 1857-1914.

                        The book is part of the Historical studies (Muslim India) series and was published in 2006 by Adam Publishers & Distributors.

                        493

                        Which community is facing target killing in Baluchistan?

                        Correct Answer: Hazara
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Targeted suicide attack on the Hazara community killed 79 people.

                        The Hazaras claim that there have been 173 attacks on their community

                        494

                        The Battle of Saragarhi in Afghanistan is names as _____?

                        Correct Answer: Last-Stand Battle
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        The Battle of Saragarhi was fought on 12 September 1897.

                        The Battle of Saragarhi was fought between the British Punjabi Soldiers and Afghan tribesmen

                        The Battle of Saragarhi was a last-stand battle fought before the Tirah Campaign 

                        On 12 September 1897, an estimated 12,000 – 24,000 Orakzai and Afridi tribesmen were seen near Gogra, at Samana Suk

                        ND06-08-2023


                        495

                        Which barrage is not present in Sindh Province?

                        Correct Answer: Taunsa Barrage
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Sindh is a province of Pakistan.

                        Sindh is known as Bab-ul-Islam

                        Sindh has 30 Districts and 7 district

                        The mountain range located in Sindh is Kirthar Range

                        There are three Barrages in Sindh

                        1. Guddu Barrage

                        foundation-stone of Guddu Barrage on 2 February 1957.

                        2. Kotri Barrage

                        it was completed in 1955.

                        3. Sukkur Barrage

                        it was built during the British Raj from 1923 to 1932 and was named Lloyd Barrage

                        ND14-3-2023


                        496

                        Who was the only Hindu Courtier of Akbar who accepted Din-i-Ilahi?

                        Correct Answer: Birbal
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Raja Birbal was the only Hindu to adopt Din-i-Ilahi, the religion founded by Akbar.

                        The Mughal Emperor who promulgated Din-i-Ilahi in 1581 was Akbar.

                        Akbar was born in 1542.

                        He was died in 1605.

                        He was born in Sindh.

                        Akbar ruled as the Mughal Emperor from 1556 to 1605.

                        Akbar is buried in Agra.

                        Akbar was the third Mughal Emperor.

                        Muhammad Shah was the last Mughal emperor to sit on the Peacock Throne.

                        497

                        Tahzeeb-ul-Akhlaq magazine published in ______?

                        Correct Answer: 1870
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Tehzeeb-ul-Akhlaq is a journal written by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan.

                        Tehzeeb-ul-Ikhlaq established in 1871.

                        The same journal was restarted by Syed Hamid Vice-chancellor of Aligarh Muslim University in 1981.

                        First Time started from 1871 till 1961

                        Restarted in 1981–present.

                        498

                        Shah Wali Ullah persuaded Ahmad Shah Abdali to fight against?

                        Correct Answer: Marhatas
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Shah Wali Ullah (1703-1762) persuaded Ahmad Shah Abdali,

                        He was the founder of the Durrani Empire, to invade India and fight against the Marathas,The 

                        Marathas were a powerful Hindu empire at that time.

                        In 1761, Ahmad Shah Abdali, with the support of Shah Wali Ullah, defeated the Marathas in the Third Battle of Panipat.

                        499

                        Which city of Pakistan is known as Paris of Asia?

                        Correct Answer: Karachi
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Karachi is the largest city of Pakistan.

                        Karachi is the capital of Sindh. 

                        One British author described Karachi as being 'the Paris of Asia.

                        500

                        1962 constitution of Pakistan lasted for how many years?

                        Correct Answer: 7 years
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        The 1962 constitution was remained active for 7 years (1962-1969)

                        President Ayub Khan promulgated the Constitution of 1962 on 1 March 1962

                        It finally came into effect on 8 June 1962

                        In 1962 constitution:

                        • The presidential government system was introduced 
                        • Urdu and Bengali was Declared as the Official Language 

                        Iskandar Ali Mirza, the first president of Pakistan abrogated the constitution of 1956 


                        Ayoub Khan was made chief of martial law after this abrogation


                        Ayoub Khan enforced the new constitution on 8th June 1962


                        Yahiya Khan abrogated the 1962 constitution and became CMLA

                        Nice work getting this far. Consistency beats intensity — come back tomorrow and keep stacking sets.
